title: CLAUDE.md вАФ ackwards
text: ackwards R package implementing GoldbergвАЩs (2006) bass-ackwards method modern descendants (PCA / EFA / ESEM engines) hierarchical structural analysis. Extract solutions 1..k factors, characterize hierarchy via -level factor-score correlations. Full rationale, contracts, object spec, resolved defaults DESIGN.md. Note: Forbes (2023) footnote 3 cites package (github.com/jmgirard/ackwards) reference implementation extended bass-ackwards approach. Fidelity paperвАЩs algorithm baseline contract anything Forbes-related; additive enrichments acceptable default output must reproduce ForbesвАЩs examples exactly. contract test-backed (M44): tests/testthat/test-forbes-fidelity.R reproduces paperвАЩs three simulation studies expected values computed ForbesвАЩs reference implementation (OSF pcwm8; provenance fixture), M44 feasibility study additionally verified 155-variable AMH applied example 3.9e-14 (committed test вАФ AMH matrix carries OSF license; see ROADMAP.md unscheduled items).

title: CLAUDE.md вАФ ackwards
text: encode hard-won reasoning design phase. Changing design decision, refactor. One edge path. -level correlations go compute_edges(). Use algebra (W'RW, standardized) scoring linear; materialize scores nonlinear (EAP) user asks. Always standardize real score SDs sqrt(diag(W'RW)) вАФ never assume unit variance (Bartlett/oblique scores unit-variance). Keep cross-check. Retain scores route even algebra default, keep test asserting agree within tolerance linear engines. Light core, heavy opt-. object always carries loadings/variance/fit/weights/edges/ lineage/R/meta. scores, raw fits, raw data NULL default recomputable. Sign alignment anchors primary parent, вАЬpositiveвАЭ (вАЩs impossible). Lineage lives edges, never IDs. m{k}f{j} stable labels; parentage edge structure. Loud defaults. Announce consequential auto-choices via cli (e.g., ordinal-detection warning). Advise loudly; never switch basis silently. Convergence data, error. non-converging level warns + skipped; object still builds deepest converged level. Never let one bad level abort run.

title: CLAUDE.md вАФ ackwards
text: R >= 4.1 (native pipe |> \(x) lambdas allowed). Standard devtools loop: Efficiency (suite takes minutes вАФ donвАЩt re-run needlessly). check() already runs full test suite examples rebuilds vignettes (~3 min: ~90s tests + ~74s vignettes + ~20s examples), covr::package_coverage() runs suite вАФ test() вЖТ check() вЖТ coverage() one gate executes suite ~3√Ч. Instead: iterate targeted devtools::test(filter = "<x>") / testthat::test_file(); run failing tests way shows details (capture res <- use non-silent reporter вАФ never silent--rerun); skip vignette rebuild mid-work checks check(vignettes = FALSE); final gate run check() (subsumes test()) coverage() . Never run two package-touching R processes concurrently. Scaffolding helpers: usethis::use_r(), use_test(), use_package(), use_testthat(3), use_github_action("check-standard"). Use testthat 3e, roxygen2 exported functions (document default, runnable @examples, @seealso cross-links).
code: devtools::load_all()      # load for interactive testing devtools::document()      # regenerate roxygen docs + NAMESPACE after any roxygen change devtools::test()          # run testthat suite devtools::check()         # full R CMD check styler::style_pkg()       # format lintr::lint_package()     # lint

title: CLAUDE.md вАФ ackwards
text: DonвАЩt commit milestone work (anything touching R/, tests/, DESCRIPTION, vignettes) straight master. Trivial isolated doc fixes may go direct userвАЩs discretion вАФ push immediately. unpushed commit master leaves local ahead origin/master; git pull wonвАЩt surface (nothing fetch), next milestone branch вАФ cut local master вАФ bundle milestoneвАЩs squash-merge force post-merge divergence. /plan-milestone step 8a now guards branching ahead--origin master. Exception вАФ release / CRAN-submission milestones (e.g.¬†CRAN-prep, version-bump/release): wait full green CI matrix merging, CRAN runs exactly matrix (macOS/Windows/Ubuntu √Ч release/devel/oldrel) reject platform failures local macOS check() canвАЩt see. , donвАЩt merge local-green alone. exception also reactivates package real users collaborators (red master blocks others / ships bugs) вАФ treat transition trigger reconsider branch protection. touch legacy branch v0-legacy tag вАФ preserve pre-AI code. Small, focused commits imperative messages (e.g., Add PCA engine level contract). DonвАЩt force-push master. DonвАЩt commit data, credentials, large binaries.

title: DESIGN.md вАФ Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Implement GoldbergвАЩs (2006) bass-ackwards method modern descendants: extract series factor/component solutions 1 k, characterize hierarchy correlations factor/component scores adjacent (, extensions, ) levels. package provides multiple extraction engines, principled tooling choosing k, sign/lineage-aware naming, tidy outputs, attractive console + graphical reporting.

title: DESIGN.md вАФ Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Prior art. psych::bassAckward() (Revelle) already implements core loop PCA EFA rotation tenBerge scores. reimplement factor extraction numerics; wrap established engines add psych . contribution. - first-class ESEM engine (lavaan), bringing Mplus psychopathology/HiTOP workflow R вАФ per-level fit indices SEs, ordinal/Likert support вАФ psych::bassAckward provide. (Kim & Eaton 2015; Forbush et al.¬†2024 reference workflows.) - Proper ordinal/Likert handling (polychoric basis, ordinal estimators). - Forbes (2023) extended method (redundancy + artifact pruning via standalone prune() verb, -levels correlations). - replicability gate hierarchy depth: split-half factor comparability (Everett 1983; Goldberg 1990 вАФ method inventorвАЩs quality gate, dropped modern ESEM/HiTOP lineage) standalone comparability() verb (M46; ¬І14.35), recommended end--end workflow documented vignette("ackwards-girard"). - clean, tidy, serializable result object print/summary/tidy/glance/autoplot. - lineage-aligned layered diagram rather misleading tree. title: DESIGN.md вАФ Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: scoring scheme scores linear map standardized observed variables, S = Z W, -level score correlations exactly: scores need materialized вАФ R weight matrices. Waller (2007) derived orthogonal components, identity holds linear W, includes regression (Thurstone, W = R^{-1} ќЫ), Bartlett, tenBerge scores. standard PCA EFA/ESEM (regression-scored) paths algebra-eligible. Standardization trap. Component/factor scores unit-variance general (Bartlett oblique scores especially). Always divide real score SDs sqrt(diag(W'RW)). Assuming unit variance silently corrupts correlations.
code: cor(S_a, S_b) = D_a^{-1/2} (W_a' R W_b) D_b^{-1/2}            where  R   = input correlation matrix (pearson or polychoric)                   D_x = diag(W_x' R W_x)   # score variances; NOT assumed 1

title: DESIGN.md вАФ Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
code: scoring <- list(   linear    = TRUE,                # are scores a fixed linear map S = Z W?   method    = "tenBerge",          # "components" | "regression" | "bartlett" | "tenBerge" | "EAP" | ...   basis     = "pearson",           # "pearson" | "polychoric" | "spearman"   weights   = W,                   # p x k score-coefficient matrix; NULL if !linear   score_var = v                    # length-k vector = diag(W'RW); NULL if !linear )

title: DESIGN.md вАФ Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Two situations force scores route: 1. Nonlinear scoring вАФ EAP/MAP/ML scores categorical/ordinal ESEM. (ordinal ESEM instead uses regression scores polychoric loadings, stays linear вЖТ algebra polychoric R.) 2. User wants empirical, sample-realized correlations (including FIML/missing-data realities) rather model-implied quantity. missingness differ; document one вАЬedgeвАЭ represents (default: model-consistent / algebra eligible).
code: compute_edges(levels, R,               edge_method = c("auto", "algebra", "scores"),               pairs  = c("adjacent", "all"),   # "all" for Forbes extension               data   = NULL,               align  = TRUE,               use    = "pairwise") {    for (each pair (a, b) in chosen pairs) {      algebra_ok <- levels[[a]]$scoring$linear &&                   levels[[b]]$scoring$linear && !is.null(R)      if (edge_method == "algebra" || (edge_method == "auto" && algebra_ok)) {       Wa <- levels[[a]]$scoring$weights       Wb <- levels[[b]]$scoring$weights       C  <- crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wb)                       # W_a' R W_b       sa <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wa, R %*% Wa)))           # score SDs       sb <- sqrt(diag(crossprod(Wb, R %*% Wb)))       E  <- sweep(sweep(C, 1, sa, "/"), 2, sb, "/")       # standardize     } else {       stopifnot(!is.null(data))                           # nonlinear/EAP or edge_method="scores"       Sa <- score(levels[[a]], data); Sb <- score(levels[[b]], data)       E  <- cor(Sa, Sb, use = use)     }      if (align) E <- align_signs(E, lineage)               # see ¬І7     edges[[pair]] <- E   }   # return: list of (k_a x k_b) matrices  +  tidy edge tibble (from, to, r, is_primary, above_cut) }

Light tidy core always; heavy artifacts opt-. - Always: loadings, variance, fit/convergence, weight matrices, within-level correlations, edges, lineage, p x p input R, meta. enough diagram + interpretation small shareable. - Opt-: factor scores (O(n √Ч ќ£k); also privacy/sharing liability), raw fits (lavaan fits can large), raw data. Defaults: scores = FALSE, keep_fits = FALSE, keep R = TRUE. directly answers вАЬgiant list fitted models vs.¬†just loadings/scores/correlationsвАЭ question: neither вАФ structured light core, heavy bits nullable recomputable.

title: DESIGN.md вАФ Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: IDs: models numbered factor count (m1вА¶mK); factors within model m{k}f{j}. Deterministic within-level order = primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak. Sort factors within level (primary parentвАЩs position level , descending variance). single top factor ties fall back variance. places child parent splits read cleanly; f{j} ID order follows layout order tables plots agree. Limitation: ordering reduces canвАЩt eliminate crossings (child strong secondary parent elsewhere still throws crossing edge); layoutвАЩs barycenter step (¬І11) refines x-position continuously beyond discrete order. Lineage lives edges/lineage, never ID. child can multiple strong parents (point method), donвАЩt encode parentage m{k}f{j} вАФ breaks structure isnвАЩt clean tree. Matching: assign factor primary parent via greedy per-column argmax |r| (.e.¬†apply(abs(E), 2, .max)). Multiple children sharing parent normal expected вАФ adjacent levels always n_b = n_a + 1 (level k k factors vs kвИТ1 ), pigeonhole least two children share parent. bijection (LSAP/Hungarian) therefore ill-posed removed. TuckerвАЩs ѕЖ serves supplementary congruence criterion redundancy_phi filtering (Forbes extension, ¬І14.19) used matching . Sign alignment вАФ anchor primary parent, вАЬpositive.вАЭ Sign one DoF per factor; make every cross-level correlation positive. Rule: anchor m1f1 defined orientation (positive sum loadings, substantive marker item), orient factor correlation primary parent positive, propagating top-. Document non-primary edges may legitimately negative. Implementation note (M35): вАЬpropagating top-вАЭ means childвАЩs flip chosen parentвАЩs already-aligned sign, parentвАЩs raw orientation вАФ otherwise flipped parent leaves primary edge displaying negative. every primary-parent edge non-negative; secondary edges may red.

text: suggest_k() returns several complementary criteria consensus range, never single number. five criteria implemented (M12): Selective computation (M25). criteria argument (rlang::arg_match(multiple = TRUE), default five) lets callers request subset c("pa_pc", "pa_fa", "map", "vss", "cd"). Skipping real compute saving, output filtering: pa_pc/pa_fa share one fa.parallel(fa = "") call (neither run) map/vss share one vss() call, e.g.¬†criteria = "map" avoids PA simulation entirely; vss toggles VSS-1+VSS-2 unit. Non-requested k_* fields NA; criteria data frame keeps stable schema (NA-filled non-run columns); object records criteria_requested. print()/autoplot() render requested criteria consensus range computed requested criteria . Empirical Kaiser Criterion (EKC) EGA (EGAnet) scope вАФ additional deps without sufficient incremental benefit five criteria . Note: suggest_k() accepts cor = "pearson" (default) "spearman" ; "polychoric" supported (PA VSS polychoric eigen-decomposition path). Users analysing ordinal data run suggest_k() Pearson matrix switch cor = "polychoric" ackwards(). Report k maximum depth; users often deliberately set level two past consensus watch factors fragment. Note overextraction/non-replicability caution (Forbes 2023). Add seed argument reproducibility CD step вАФ psych::fa.parallel() respond reliably set.seed(), PA simulation results vary across calls regardless seed. See vignette("ackwards-suggest-k") narrative/educational treatment five criteria including pros/cons, bias direction, engine--criterion pairing (M14). suggest_k() answers вАЬdepth range plausibleвАЭ eigenstructure. companion question вАФ вАЬfactors actually replicateвАЭ вАФ answered directly standalone comparability() verb (M46, ¬І14.35): split-half factor comparability per level per factor, depth floor suggest_k()вАЩs ceiling. two deliberately separate functions: suggest_k() pre-fit screen eigenvalue criteria, comparability() engine-specific fit-based.

title: DESIGN.md вАФ Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis (R package)
text: Resolved (design round): 1. Rotation вЖТ varimax (orthogonal) across engines, hardcoded internal constant since M13; oblique rotation scope (resolved 2026-06 вАФ confounds cross-level signal methodвАЩs whole point). rotation removed user argument M13. 2. Correlation basis вЖТ pearson default, polychoric opt-, ordinal-detection cli warning (silent switching). 3. Within-level order вЖТ primary-parent (recursive), variance tiebreak; f{j} IDs follow . 4. Ordinal ESEM scoring вЖТ tenBerge polychoric (linear вЖТ algebra) default; EAP opt-. Default Likert path need data edge time. 5. Package name вЖТ ackwards (verify via available::available("ackwards")). Resolved build (M1вАУM3): 6. Ordinal-detection heuristic: вЙ§ 7 distinct integer values triggers warning. 7. CF kappa: ќЇ = 1/p (varimax-equivalent; Crawford & Ferguson 1970). kappa argument accepted stored never wired engine вАФ engines hardcoded cfT вЖТ "varimax". Removed entirely M13: CF(ќЇ=1/p) вЙ° varimax; literature never varies kappa; exposing implied quartimax/equamax reasonable alternatives (method). 8. cor = "spearman" added alongside "pearson" non-polychoric rank-based option. 9. fm argument exposed EFA engine ("minres" default, "ml", "pa"). 10. cut_show argument exposed (default 0.3) control edges flagged above_cut. Resolved M4: 11. ESEM engine вЖТ lavaan::efa() (EFA--SEM, full ESEM structural paths). Gives per-level fit indices + rotation-aware SEs + WLSMV ordinal estimation. require full ESEM block syntax; bass-ackwards levels independent EFA solutions. 12. ESEM scoring вЖТ self-compute tenBerge weights lavaanвАЩs estimated loadings ќЫ latent correlation matrix R via existing .tenBerge_weights(R, ќЫ). lavPredict() used default path (lacks tenBerge); eventual hook EAP opt-. 13. EAP scoring вЖТ scope (declined M28). EAPвАЩs shrinkage attenuates cross-level correlations вАФ primary signal bass-ackwards measures вАФ making theoretically inferior tenBerge method. EAP request still returns cli_abort("yet implemented"). scores-route seam compute_edges() preserved, implementing EAP planned. 14. cor = "polychoric" вЖТ general basis engines (ESEM-). PCA/EFA compute R via psych::polychoric() ackwards() feed engine usual. ESEM uses lavaanвАЩs latent correlation matrix R edges (mix psych-polychoric R lavaan-WLSMV loadings). 15. ESEM ordinal estimator вЖТ WLSMV default; ULSMV documented option. 16. loadings_se вЖТ added ¬І4 level contract (p√Чk matrix, NULL PCA/EFA). Completed M4 (addition items 11вАУ16 ): - NPD polychoric matrices: warn + smooth via psych::cor.smooth() (implemented). - Convergence truncation tested ESEM engine (k=4+ p=6 triggers lavaan error вЖТ warn + truncate вЖТ object builds k_eff=3). - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check documented Pearson/continuous paths ; polychoric ESEM edges (algebra uses lavaan polychoric R; scores route uses Pearson standardization) diverge design excluded oracle. Resolved M5 (Forbes extension): 17. API вЖТ two orthogonal args: pairs = c("adjacent","") prune = c("none","redundant","artefact") (char vector; default "none"). prune != "none" auto-upgrades pairs "" loud cli_inform() (chains need -levels edges assess). Superseded M34 (item 27 ): pruning now standalone prune() verb, ackwards() argument, pairs longer auto-upgrades. 18. Prune action вЖТ flag-, never remove. Adds pruned/prune_reason annotation columns edge/node tidy structures; object retains levels (preserves Invariant 5 algebra-vs-scores oracle). Pruning interpretive relabeling, re-estimation вАФ say print/docs (extends ¬І2 honesty caveat). 19. Redundancy вЖТ faithful Forbes (2023): score-correlation chains |r| вЙ• .9 (default, tunable), retention rule = keep bottom node chain reaches level k (-specific, best-defined), else keep topmost node (broadest manifestation). TuckerвАЩs ѕЖ (> .95, Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge 2006) computed aligned loadings optional conjunctive criterion. ѕЖ formula ќ£aбµҐbбµҐ / sqrt(ќ£aбµҐ¬≤ ¬Ј ќ£bбµҐ¬≤); base R, new dependency. 20. Additive enrichments paper (default output still matches ForbesвАЩs examples): always report r ѕЖ every redundancy candidate (report-first, flag-second вАФ borderline cases like paperвАЩs .89/.93 alcohol component stay visible); report endpoint r (direct, -levels edges) alongside chain flag disagree (correlation non-transitive: clean adjacent chain neither implies implied endpoint identity вАФ chain answers вАЬperpetuates every level,вАЭ endpoint r answers вАЬconstructвАЭ). 21. Artifact вЖТ never auto-flagged. prune(x, "artifact") surfaces ѕЖ inspection; removal documented researcher judgment (Forbes explicit introduces researcher DoF / confirmation bias; cf.¬†Wicherts et al.¬†2016). Resolved M32 (API-shape & naming; owner-reviewed, equivalent guidance elsewhere document): 22. tidy(x, = "fit") long-format key column вЖТ statistic, replacing index (read row position; actually held fit-index names EFA/ESEM eigenvalue positions PCA). Wide format (format = "wide") unaffected вАФ column names still come values key. 23. k_max naming collision (ackwards() = extraction depth vs.¬†suggest_k() = max factors/components evaluated) вЖТ keep shared name functions. вАЩre genuinely dial applied different workflow stages (suggest_k(k_max = ...) вЖТ ackwards(k_max = ...)); renaming one create vocabulary drift without removing real ambiguity. Resolved instead via roxygen: functionвАЩs @param k_max states meaning cross-references вАЩs. 24. tidy(= "fit") cutoff flags вЖТ removed. cutoffs = TRUE argument meets/{statistic}_meets columns produced (.flag_fit()) dropped; pass/fail boolean quietly endorsed Hu & Bentler (1999) thresholds package elsewhere (¬І9, section) treats conventional contested вАФ continuing M28/M31 output-honesty trajectory. .fit_cutoffs() retained internal helper: autoplot(= "fit") still draws threshold reference lines summary() still annotates inline check/cross mark: visual guides, returned pass/fail column user mistake computed judgment. 25. Variance scale вЖТ proportion, 0-1 (tidy(= "variance") columns renamed variance_pct/cumulative_pct вЖТ proportion/cumulative, values divided 100). aligns tidy() engineвАЩs internal variance slot (already 0-1; see print.ackwards, reads directly) broom/psych convention (psych reports вАЬProportion VarвАЭ 0-1). Percent formatting moves display layer (print(), summary(), vignette gt tables) rather living tidy data. 26. M31-deferred: effective ESEM estimator recorded $meta. M31 explicitly deferred (вАЬbetter bundled M32вАЩs meta/column decisions bolted вАЭ): x$meta$estimator now stores effective estimator auto-selection ("ML"/"MLR"/"WLSMV"/"ULSMV"; NA PCA/EFA). summary() gains one-line footnote naming lavaanвАЩs scaled-variant reporting (¬І14.M31 point 2/Post-review) whenever effective estimator "WLSMV"/"ULSMV"/"MLR" вАФ shown "ML", scaled variant. five M32 changes breaking deprecation path (pre-CRAN, users; consistent M34 pruning-verb precedent clean moves compatibility shims). Resolved M34 (pruning verb; owner-reviewed, breaking, deprecation path вАФ pre-CRAN, users): 27. Pruning extracted standalone, pipeable S3 generic prune() (prune.ackwards), ackwards() argument. five prune-related args (prune вЖТ renamed rules, redundancy_r, redundancy_phi, min_items, orphan_r) leave ackwards() entirely: ackwards(...) |> prune(...). Rationale: extraction expensive, deterministic step; pruning cheap interpretive. Separating lets researcher re-prune new thresholds without re-extracting (re-running ESEM per level expensive path M26 optimized). prune() generic (UseMethod), plain function, coexists prune generics already defined recursive-partitioning packages (e.g.¬†rpart::prune) regardless package load order. Returns ackwards object $prune populated (replacing prior pruning) вАФ new class, print/summary/tidy/glance/augment/ autoplot work unchanged. 28. Edges redundancy chains artifact ѕЖ recomputed fresh inside prune() via compute_edges(pairs = "") objectвАЩs stored levels/r (Invariant 3), never written back x$edges (Invariant 1: one edge path). ackwards()вАШs pairs auto-upgrade "" pruning requested (M5 item 17) removed along вАФ pairs now pure display/storage setting ackwards(), decoupled pruning; prune() works correctly regardless fit-time pairs value. 29. Manual pruning: prune(x, rules = "none", manual = c("m4f3", "m4f4")) flags user-named nodes directly (standalone, auto rule needed), unions onto auto ruleвАЩs flags (prune(x, "redundant", manual = c(...))). Unknown labels error. overlap auto rule manual, auto ruleвАЩs prune_reason wins (informative); otherwise-unflagged manual nodes get prune_reason = "manual". 30. Naming: canonical rule name "artifact" (US spelling, ownerвАЩs preference), "artefact" accepted alias (nod Commonwealth spelling ForbesвАЩ usage) normalized internally "artifact". Existing code passing "artefact" keeps working. "tucker" considered rejected alias/rename: mode surfaces TuckerвАЩs ѕЖ (also computes few_items/orphan/split_merge structural signals), naming statistic mislabel umbrella вАФ "artifact" names mode . 31. ackwards() explicitly rejects five removed args passed via ... (rather silently absorbing ) pointer prune() вАФ silent absorption masked-argument footgun, clean break intended (Invariant 6: loud, silent). Resolved M38 (missing = "fiml" PCA/EFA; reverses resolved default вАФ owner-signed-): 32. FIML promoted first-class PCA/EFA route. M16 contract вАЬmissing = "fiml" errors PCA, EFAвАЭ reversed Pearson basis: engine = "pca"/"efa" cor = "pearson", missing = "fiml" now routes R psych::corFiml() (full-information ML, MVN) feeds existing W'RW algebra вАФ Invariant-1-clean (one edge path, one corFiml call per run since R computed reused across levels; new dependency, psych already Imports). still errors non-Pearson PCA/EFA basis (Spearman, polychoric вАФ corFiml honor ) WLSMV/ULSMV. .resolve_missing() gained cor argument enforce guard matrix. Grew M37 doc-planning observation user already smuggle FIML via M22 correlation-matrix seam (ackwards(corFiml(x), вА¶)); M38 makes capability discoverable owns n_obs tradeoff. 33. n_obs string option + default. raw-data FIML PCA/EFA path, n_obs accepts "total" (default) "complete". "total" = every row contributing FIML likelihood, matching convention genuine FIML analysis reports (Enders 2010) giving cross-engine parallelism ESEM-ML FIML. Point estimates (loadings/edges) depend choice; EFA fit indices , approximate two-step (FIML matrix вЖТ normal-theory EFA) procedure regardless N (Zhang & Savalei 2020) вАФ route announces caveat via cli (Invariant 6). "effective" considered dropped: canonical formula exists corFimlвЖТEFA route, package-invented convention masquerading standard. string n_obs rejected path; cor-matrix input still requires numeric N. Resolved M45 (--sample scoring; owner-approved 2026-07-01): 34. Scoring standardizes fit-time moments default. augment()вАЩs scaling argument defaults "fit": supplied data standardized training means/SDs (stored meta$item_means/item_sds since M45) weight matrices apply. Rationale: вАЬapplying trained modelвАЭ means вАФ test observationвАЩs score must depend observations share split, train/test scores must share one metric (cross-validation use case motivated milestone). "sample" (pre-M45 behaviour) remains explicit opt-deliberately re-standardizing sample different population metric, option cor-matrix objects. Breaking subset/new-data scoring values (pre-CRAN, deprecation path; M32/M34 precedent). companion predict.ackwards() export chosen augment-surface discoverability replicators (psych/lavaan users reach predict()). Resolved M46 (Girard extension вАФ replicability gate; owner-approved 2026-07-01): 35. Split-half factor comparability standalone verb. comparability(data, k_max, engine,     cor, fm, n_splits = 10, seed) resurrects Everett (1983) / Goldberg (1990) factor comparability coefficients hierarchy-depth gate, extended every bass-ackwards level. Mechanics: full-sample ackwards() fit anchors labels (results report m{k}f{j} user see); per split, levels 1..k fit independently random half via engine internals; half-solution matched anchor greedy--removal max-|r| bijection (square, -k вАФ well-posed , unlike ¬І7 parent matching; clue dependency, near-ties instability measured); coefficient correlation two matched half-solution scores pooled correlation matrix, computed compute_edges() two-element levels list (Invariant 1 вАФ one edge path), signed orienting matched factors positively toward anchor (negative coefficient stays visible diagnostic). TuckerвАЩs ѕЖ matched loading columns reported alongside (score agreement vs.¬†pattern agreement). Report-first, judge-never: nothing auto-flagged; conventional .90/.95 benchmarks appear reference lines / footer prose (M32 cutoff philosophy). Scope: PCA/EFA (ESEM deferred вАФ 2 * n_splits lavaan hierarchies need performance treatment; logged ROADMAP.md) pearson/spearman (suggest_k() precedent: polychoric estimation every half-sample slow NPD-prone; screen Pearson, fit final model polychoric). Repeated splits default n_splits = 10 (GoldbergвАЩs practice; single split misleads luck draw). Per-half engine warnings muffled convergence shortfall summarised (summary$n_splits_ok). recommended workflow вАФ suggest_k ceiling вЖТ comparability floor вЖТ fit вЖТ prune вЖТ interpret вЖТ --sample validation вАФ documented vignette("ackwards-girard") named replicability-gated (Girard) workflow. Bootstrap edge CIs standalone verb. boot_edges(x, data, n_boot = 1000, conf = 0.95, seed) attaches nonparametric bootstrap SEs + percentile CIs every edge вАФ inferential-honesty companion point-estimate prune() rules Forbes strongest- edge practice (resurrects ¬І14 e4 deferral). standalone verb (ackwards() argument), consistent M34 direction extracting expensive/optional work pipeable verbs (prune(), comparability()); raw data re-supplied light core store (Invariant 3). Mechanics per replicate: resample rows replacement вЖТ recompute R fitвАЩs cor/missing routine вЖТ refit levels 1..k_max (engine internals, muffled) вЖТ anchor replicate level full-sample solution (item-35 greedy-max-|r| matching + sign orientation, evaluated full-sample R) вЖТ edges via compute_edges() replicate R (Invariant 1). Anchoring load-bearing: without , factor label-switching sign-flipping across replicates corrupt pooled distributions. resample indices drawn upfront seed, replicate deterministic given indices serial future.apply-parallel paths (M26 pattern) agree bit--bit вАФ design choice makes вАЬserial вЙ° parallelвАЭ test-assertable. Failed replicates drop NA counted per edge (n_boot_ok), never aborting (Invariant 7). Output attaches x$boot; tidy(= "edges") gains se/lo/hi/n_boot_ok, print/summary note coverage. Scope: PCA/EFA + pearson/spearman , mirroring item 35 (ESEM = n_boot lavaan hierarchies; polychoric = per-resample polychoric estimation, slow + NPD-prone; logged ROADMAP.md). Cor-matrix input, ESEM, polychoric objects error pointers. DESIGN ¬І14вАЩs original вАЬreuse loadings_se infrastructureвАЭ phrasing amended: loadings_se lavaanвАЩs analytic delta-method SE, analogue edges varimax-rotated hierarchies вАФ bootstrap SE method; reused storage/tidy display pattern. Percentile (Fisher-z normal-approx) CIs: percentile respects [-1, 1] bound skew r near 0.9 prune threshold. Oracle note: full-pipeline bootstrap SE legitimately exceeds Fisher-z analytic SE materialized-score correlation, replicate re-extracts factors (loading uncertainty) Fisher-z treats scores fixed observed variables; exact Fisher-z match holds fixed-weights percentile bootstrap (halves test-asserted). intervals per-edge error bars, familywise correction вАФ make edgeвАЩs precision visible undo selection bias scanning many edges strongest (stated object docs + Forbes vignette). Known limitations / deferred future milestones: - factor_cor ESEM engine permuted variance-sort ord vector. Safe permanently: orthogonal rotation supported (factor_cor = ; permutation ), oblique rotation scope (¬І9, ¬І14.1). guard comment engine_esem.R documents required decision ever reversed. - Algebra-vs-scores cross-check cover cor = "polychoric" paths (see ), missing = "fiml" PCA/EFA path (M38): algebra uses psych::corFiml() matrix scores route standardizes raw, NA-bearing data (pairwise Pearson SDs), two bases diverge missingness design вАФ reason polychoric excluded. oracle tests therefore run complete-data linear engines; FIML/polychoric object fed , false-failure risk, cross-check certify paths. - cor = "spearman" + engine = "esem" semantically inconsistent (lavaan fits Pearson ML raw data edges use Spearman R); warning now emitted (M10). - ESEM engine detect warn improper/Heywood solutions вАФ resolved M10: engine now inspects lavaan::lavInspect(fit, "theta") warns (diag(theta) <= 0) (parity EFA engine, Invariant 7). - ESEM ML/MLR missing = "pairwise": lavaan uses listwise deletion model fit edges computed separately-computed pairwise stats::cor() вАФ minor inconsistency (fit statistics complete-case N, edges full pairwise N). Documented $meta$missing; per-call advisory warning fires whenever NAs detected. Use missing = "listwise" "fiml" resolve. (Added M16; ¬І9 missing row cross-references .) - Forbes-extension improvements deferred past M5 (published method weaker spots worth strengthening later; M5 ships faithful method + ¬І14.20 reporting enrichments): - Structural artefact signals. Done M25 (Wave 2). prune = "artefact" now populates x$prune$structural per-factor few_items / orphan / split_merge signals (flag/report ; auto-pruning). Args: min_items = 3L, orphan_r = 0.5. split_merge TRUE factorвАЩs primary items came multiple different primary factors preceding level. CLI print()/summary() report flagged count. - Factor-score-indeterminacy caveat EFA/ESEM redundancy. Done M25 (Wave 3). redundancy_phi = NULL now auto-resolves: PCA вЖТ ѕЖ filter; EFA/ESEM вЖТ 0.95. NA explicit opt-. Announces via cli auto-applied (Invariant 6). See ¬І9 defaults table. - Selection bias вАЬstrongestвАЭ edge. Done M47 (item 36). boot_edges() adds nonparametric bootstrap SEs + percentile CIs every edge (PCA/EFA + pearson/spearman; upfront seeded indices вЖТ serial вЙ° parallel; full-sample anchoring per replicate; future.apply dispatch; tidy/print/summary integration; Forbes-vignette honesty section). intervals quantify per-edge precision design correct multiplicity scanning many edges strongest вАФ caveat documented, silently вАЬfixedвАЭ. - M40 spin-(code/viz deferred doc-M39; shipped M40 except declined flag): - Ordinal categorical convenience flag. Declined M40 (owner sign-, 2026-07-01). proposed categorical = TRUE/FALSE argument ackwards() flipping cor (pearsonвЖТpolychoric) ESEM estimator (ML/MLRвЖТWLSMV) together. Rejected redundant: cor = "polychoric" already auto-selects WLSMV (estimator = NULL auto-rule, ¬І9), categorical = TRUE pure synonym cor = "polychoric" вАФ two-settings--one shortcut. add conflict surface (categorical = TRUE + cor = "pearson"?) ¬І9 defaults change zero new capability. Discoverability handled docs layer instead (ordinal vignette already states cor = "polychoric" alone flips estimator; ordinal-detection cli warning names option runtime). R//DESCRIPTION/¬І9 change. - Ordinal correlation-comparison visualization. Done M40. two raw round(x$r[1:5,1:5], 2) matrix chunks vignettes/ackwards-ordinal.Rmd replaced one dodged bar chart ten N1вАУN5 lower-triangle item-pair correlations, fill = basis (Pearson vs polychoric), reshape code hidden. Viz-; ggplot2 already Suggests; package-code dependency change. (gt long-format ќФ-table alternative set aside вАФ vignette already carries two gt ќФ-tables, chart adds variety directly addresses вАЬmatrices hard compareвАЭ.) - Forbes pruned-level axis-label styling. Done M40. fully-pruned level (every factor flagged) now gets italic autoplot() axis label normal (non-drop_pruned) render path, denoting status alongside existing grey node fill; partially-pruned levels keep plain label. Automatic (new argument). See ¬І11.

title: Choosing an Engine: PCA, EFA, and ESEM
text: PCA extracts principal components вАФ linear combinations observed variables capture maximum variance, including measurement error. Every item modeled communality 1.0: components account 100% itemвАЩs variance. true latent variable model; data reduction method. bass-ackwards context, PCA natural default. fast, always converges, produces eigenvalues can guide choice k. Waller (2007) showed -level algebra (W'RW) holds exactly components, making edges algebraically exact rather approximated materialized scores. вАЬfitвАЭ PCA just eigenvalue component вАФ amount variance captures. chi-square tests, RMSEA, model rejection.
code: x_pca <- ackwards(bfi, k_max = 3, cor = "polychoric") x_pca #>  #> вФАвФА Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: polychoric #> n: 875 #> k (max): 3 #>  #> вФАвФА Levels вФАвФА #>  #> вЬФ k = 1: 1 factor, 23.2% variance #> вЬФ k = 2: 2 factors, 35.5% variance #> вЬФ k = 3: 3 factors, 44.6% variance #>  #> вФАвФА Edges вФАвФА #>  #> 5 of 8 edges have |r| вЙ• 0.3 #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. Per-level fit indices #> (EFA/ESEM) describe how well a k-factor model fits the items at that level -- #> they do not validate the edges or the hierarchy itself. tidy(x_pca, what = "fit") #>   level       statistic    value #> 1     1 eigenvalue.m1f1 5.802803 #> 2     2 eigenvalue.m2f1 5.802803 #> 3     2 eigenvalue.m2f2 3.067627 #> 4     3 eigenvalue.m3f1 5.802803 #> 5     3 eigenvalue.m3f2 3.067627 #> 6     3 eigenvalue.m3f3 2.275419

title: Choosing an Engine: PCA, EFA, and ESEM
text: EFA extracts latent factors model variance shared among items. item retains unique variance (communality < 1.0) factors explain. classical common-factor model, appropriate PCA believe items fallible indicators latent constructs rather constructs . EFA produces genuine goodness--fit indices. tell whether k factors sufficient reproduce observed correlation matrix within sampling error. RMSEA values large (> 0.10), indicating 1вАУ3 factors fully account BFI item correlations вАФ unsurprising, true structure 5 factors. Fit improves steadily k = 1 k = 3, exactly kind evidence bass-ackwards analysis designed make visible.

what: How close are EFA and PCA loadings?
title: Choosing an Engine: PCA, EFA, and ESEM
text: clean, continuous data moderate--strong factor structure, EFA PCA loadings highly correlated identical. EFA loadings systematically somewhat smaller model common variance; PCA inflates loadings fitting noise alongside signal. table compares primary loadings вАФ loading item dominant factor вАФ six representative items (two Neuroticism, Extraversion, Conscientiousness families) k = 3. ќФ column teaching point: much smaller EFA loadings absolute value measurement error partitioned uniqueness. Using |EFA| вИТ |PCA| keeps attenuation consistently negative regardless loading sign. EFA loadings items consistently points lower вАФ PCA loadings include noise variance EFA partitions uniqueness. factor structure (items define factor) unchanged.

what: ESEM: EFA with full model diagnostics
title: Choosing an Engine: PCA, EFA, and ESEM
text: ESEM (exploratory structural equation modeling, Asparouhov & Muth√©n, 2009) fits common-factor model EFA uses lavaan engine. ESEM ordinal-tool: handles continuous items maximum-likelihood estimators (ML, MLR вАФ default continuous data) ordinal items WLSMV, unlocks three capabilities EFA provide: Standard errors every loading, enabling confidence intervals significance tests вАФ continuous ordinal data. Full maximum-likelihood estimation continuous data (ML/MLR), including FIML missing data (missing = "fiml"), uses partially observed rows rather deleting . WLSMV estimator ordinal data, appropriate maximum-likelihood-adjacent estimator categorical indicators. cor = "polychoric" set engine = "esem", WLSMV used automatically. ESEM fit indices include CFI SRMR addition RMSEA TLI, giving richer picture model adequacy. See вАЬPer-level fitвАЭ section report interpret indices.

code: set.seed(1) x_boot <- ackwards(bfi, k_max = 5, engine = "efa", pairs = "all") |>   boot_edges(bfi, n_boot = 200, seed = 1) #> Warning: ! 25 columns look like ordinal/Likert items (<= 7 distinct integer values): #>   "A1", "A2", "A3", "A4", "A5", "C1", вА¶, "O4", and "O5". #> вДє Results use a "pearson" basis. Consider `cor = "polychoric"` for ordinal #>   data. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> вДє Fitting 200 bootstrap replicates (EFA, k = 1-5)... #> вЬФ Fitting 200 bootstrap replicates (EFA, k = 1-5)... [39.5s] #>   boot_tbl <- tidy(x_boot, what = "edges", sort = "strength") skip_boot <- boot_tbl[   abs(boot_tbl$level_to - boot_tbl$level_from) > 1 & abs(boot_tbl$r) >= 0.5,   c("from", "to", "r", "lo", "hi") ] head(skip_boot, 6) #>    from   to         r        lo        hi #> 4  m3f2 m5f1 0.9897391 0.9673909 0.9973950 #> 6  m2f2 m4f2 0.9759641 0.9183303 0.9932987 #> 7  m2f2 m5f1 0.9733982 0.9138369 0.9901712 #> 11 m3f3 m5f3 0.8960495 0.6732509 0.9768403 #> 14 m2f1 m4f1 0.8806961 0.7226635 0.9256204 #> 15 m1f1 m3f1 0.7974610 0.7248725 0.8533350

Similarity measured TuckerвАЩs congruence coefficient (ѕЖ): ѕХ(Fa,Fb)=вИСiќїiaќїibвИСiќїia2вЛЕвИСiќїib2\phi(F_a, F_b) = \frac{\sum_i \lambda_{ia}\lambda_{ib}} {\sqrt{\sum_i \lambda_{ia}^2 \cdot \sum_i \lambda_{ib}^2}} ѕЖ ranges вИТ1 +1, values 0.95 conventionally read near-identical loading patterns regardless sign (Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge, 2006). Unlike "redundant", artifact mode never flags anything automatically. Forbes (2023) explicit identifying artifact requires researcher judgment вАФ automating manufacture investigator degrees freedom вАФ prune(x, "artifact") computes stores evidence weigh: TuckerвАЩs ѕЖ every cross-level factor pair x$prune$phi, plus structural signals next section x$prune$structural. table read x$prune$phi. natural first cut non-adjacent pairs highest |ѕЖ| вАФ deep factor whose loading pattern nearly duplicates factor two levels classic candidate: data strongest non-adjacent congruence |ѕЖ| = 0.99. Values near 1 mean deeper factor recycles earlier loading pattern; whether makes rotation artifact вАФ faithfully persisting construct, redundancy view fact вАФ judgment made substantive content items view, threshold package applies . two modes surface different fingerprints underlying question: "redundant": factor appears multiple levels near-identical score correlations вАФ persists unchanged k increases. Auto-flagged (ForbesвАЩs retention rule), score-correlation chain sharp, replicable criterion. "artifact": factorвАЩs loading pattern closely resembles factor elsewhere hierarchy, structure looks -identified (next section). Reported вАФ call .

Forbes (2023, Fig. 2) describes several structural signatures, prune(x, "artifact") reports three per factor x$prune$structural: few_items вАФ factor primary (highest-loading) home fewer min_items items (default 3). factor anchored one two items -identified often extraction artifact rather replicable construct. orphan вАФ factorвАЩs strongest correlation immediately neighbouring levels orphan_r (default 0.5). connect solutions either side, replicate across hierarchy. split_merge вАФ factorвАЩs primary items spread across two different parent factors level . Items separated coarser solution merged one factor finer solution вАФ split--merge anomaly Forbes Fig. 2. Like TuckerвАЩs ѕЖ, signals flag--report вАФ prune() never removes factor basis. Identifying artifact requires researcher judgment (Forbes explicit step introduces investigator degrees freedom); signals simply point factors worth closer look. two thresholds, min_items orphan_r, arguments prune().

code: thresholds <- c(0.80, 0.85, 0.90, 0.95) counts <- sapply(thresholds, function(thr) {   x <- prune(x_all, "redundant", redundancy_r = thr)   sum(tidy(x, what = "nodes")$pruned) }) #> вДє Redundancy pruning (|r| вЙ• 0.8) flagged 9 nodes. #> вДє Nodes are retained in the object; inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #>   `x$prune$chains`. #> вДє Redundancy pruning (|r| вЙ• 0.85) flagged 8 nodes. #> вДє Nodes are retained in the object; inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #>   `x$prune$chains`. #> вДє Redundancy pruning (|r| вЙ• 0.9) flagged 6 nodes. #> вДє Nodes are retained in the object; inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #>   `x$prune$chains`. #> вДє Redundancy pruning (|r| вЙ• 0.95) flagged 6 nodes. #> вДє Nodes are retained in the object; inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #>   `x$prune$chains`. thr_df <- data.frame(redundancy_r = thresholds, n_flagged = counts)

what: The problem: depth is where bass-ackwards analyses go wrong
title: Replicability-Gated Hierarchies: A Recommended Workflow
text: bass-ackwards analysis asks two questions: structure domain organize hierarchically, deep hierarchy meaningful? first question method computes. second published applications often stumble, failure mode consistent: overextraction, followed substantive interpretation deep-level factors re-emerge new sample. Forbes (2023) documents directly bass-ackwards context вАФ non-replicable structure concentrates deeper levels overextracted hierarchy. standard tooling half-addresses . Retention criteria like parallel analysis (suggest_k()) estimate plausible range k eigenstructure, right first screen. measure thing caution actually : whether specific factors solution show another sample population. level can sit comfortably inside plausible range still contain factor pure sample idiosyncrasy. , historically, direct instrument exactly . Everett (1983) proposed factor comparability coefficients: split sample half, fit solution half independently, apply halvesвАЩ scoring weights full sample, correlate matched factor scores. factor real re-emerges halves two score estimates correlate near 1; factor noise . GoldbergвАЩs lab used routine gate вАФ Goldberg (1990) retained factor solutions held across repeated random split-halves вАФ practice runs research program produced bass-ackwards method (Goldberg, 2006). modern bass-ackwards literature largely dropped . comparability() restores gate first-class verb, extended bass-ackwards setting: coefficients every factor every level, computed score-correlation algebra hierarchyвАЩs edges.

title: Replicability-Gated Hierarchies: A Recommended Workflow

title: Replicability-Gated Hierarchies: A Recommended Workflow
text: step workflow named . evaluate every level one past ceiling, across ten random split-halves: Read per-level minima, medians: level interpretable least replicable factor. every factor k = 5 clears conventional floor (weakest median comparability levels .92), structure degrades past вАФ k = 7, m7f7 reaches median .58 dips -.24 worst split. deep factors stable dimensions data; different factors every half-sample happen occupy slot.  Two honesty notes. First, conventional benchmarks marked plot (.90 .95, following EverettвАЩs GoldbergвАЩs practice) conventions, tests вАФ comparability() reports every coefficient flags nothing, borderline cases stay visible judgment stays . Second, comparability internal replication: asks whether structure re-emerges halves sample. right gate вАЬfactor real ,вАЭ necessary condition вАФ demonstration вАФ replication new population. result hierarchy floor: deepest level every factor replicates. data k = 5 вАФ Big Five, .

Consider `cor = "polychoric"` for ordinal #>   data. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> вДє Fitting 10 split-half replicates (PCA, k = 1-7)... #> вЬФ Fitting 10 split-half replicates (PCA, k = 1-7)... what: Step 4: Find what perpetuates without differentiating вАФ prune()
title: Replicability-Gated Hierarchies: A Recommended Workflow
text: Replicability necessary sufficient: factor can re-emerge every half-sample still add nothing, construct restated level level. ForbesвАЩs (2023) redundancy question, prune()вАЩs job, comparability()вАЩs: chains tell us several Big Five arrive early simply persist вАФ example one factor emerges k = 2 travels essentially unchanged (r вЙ• .97 every link) k = 5. Every node chain replicates beautifully; chain flags intermediate appearances add resolution. two verbs answer different questions, reporting gives honest picture: levels real, levels new. See vignette("ackwards-forbes") chain mechanics, thresholds, retention rule.

title: Replicability-Gated Hierarchies: A Recommended Workflow
text: Interpreting non-replicable deep factors. classic failure. Without replicability instrument deep levels just , printed authority robust ones. Step 2 makes status measurable visible. Trusting single split. One split-half can flatter slander factor luck draw. comparability() defaults ten random splits reports spread; minima across splits informative medians. Cherry-picking strongest edge. pairs = "", number -level correlations grows fast, reporting dramatic one capitalizes chance. Report full edge tables (complete diagram), treat вАЬstrongest linkвАЭ claim descriptive rather inferential. boot_edges() attaches bootstrap confidence interval every edge, makes estimateвАЩs precision visible вАФ useful judging whether edge clears prune()вАЩs redundancy threshold вАФ note per-edge intervals undo selection bias scanning many edges largest; see vignette("ackwards-forbes"). Mistaking persistence structure. chain near-1.0 correlations hierarchy looks impressive means levels restating one construct. prune() finding, shorten reported k range, lengthen list constructs. Mistaking replicability validity. comparability .99 means factor stable feature items population вАФ good construct, generalizes beyond population sampled. Cross-population replication external validation remain separate, subsequent steps.

what: Summary: the workflow in six calls
title: Replicability-Gated Hierarchies: A Recommended Workflow
text: suggest_k(data) вАФ screen plausible depth range; treat top ceiling probe. comparability(data, k_max = ceiling + 1) вАФ find hierarchy floor: deepest level every factor replicates across split-halves. ackwards(data, k_max = floor) вАФ fit hierarchy gated depth. prune(x, "redundant") вАФ identify factors perpetuate without differentiating; report levels add resolution. top_items(x) + autoplot(x) вАФ interpret sign, naming, descriptive-hierarchy guardrails. predict(x_train, newdata = test) вАФ validate downstream claims held-data fit-time metric. Steps 1, 3, 4, 5 established method. Step 2 restores gate methodвАЩs inventor applied , Step 6 extends logic вАФ show holds data fit вАФ whatever scores.

code: summary(x) #>  #> вФАвФА Summary: Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: polychoric #> n: 875 #> k (max): 5 #>  #> вФАвФА Levels вФАвФА #>  #> k = 1: 1 factor (23.2% cumulative variance) #> m1f1 23.21% eigenvalue 5.8 #> k = 2: 2 factors (35.5% cumulative variance) #> m2f1 20.94% eigenvalue 5.8 #> m2f2 14.54% eigenvalue 3.07 #> k = 3: 3 factors (44.6% cumulative variance) #> m3f1 18.04% eigenvalue 5.8 #> m3f2 13.89% eigenvalue 3.07 #> m3f3 12.66% eigenvalue 2.28 #> k = 4: 4 factors (52.2% cumulative variance) #> m4f1 17.5% eigenvalue 5.8 #> m4f2 13.63% eigenvalue 3.07 #> m4f3 11.83% eigenvalue 2.28 #> m4f4 9.21% eigenvalue 1.9 #> k = 5: 5 factors (58.4% cumulative variance) #> m5f1 13.75% eigenvalue 5.8 #> m5f2 13.56% eigenvalue 3.07 #> m5f3 11.9% eigenvalue 2.28 #> m5f4 10.09% eigenvalue 1.9 #> m5f5 9.12% eigenvalue 1.56 #>  #> вФАвФА Lineage (primary parents) вФАвФА #>  #> m1f1 вЖТ m2f1, m2f2 #> m2f1 вЖТ m3f1, m3f3 #> m2f2 вЖТ m3f2 #> m3f1 вЖТ m4f1 #> m3f2 вЖТ m4f2 #> m3f3 вЖТ m4f3, m4f4 #> m4f1 вЖТ m5f1, m5f4 #> m4f2 вЖТ m5f2 #> m4f3 вЖТ m5f3 #> m4f4 вЖТ m5f5 #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. Per-level fit indices #> (EFA/ESEM) describe how well a k-factor model fits the items at that level -- #> they do not validate the edges or the hierarchy itself. # Primary-parent edges, weakest last: the bottom rows are where structure shifts tidy(x, what = "edges", primary_only = TRUE, sort = "strength") |> tail() #>    from   to level_from level_to         r is_primary above_cut #> 9  m4f1 m5f1          4        5 0.8377659       TRUE      TRUE #> 10 m3f3 m4f3          3        4 0.7316162       TRUE      TRUE #> 11 m3f3 m4f4          3        4 0.6802343       TRUE      TRUE #> 12 m4f1 m5f4          4        5 0.5458269       TRUE      TRUE #> 13 m2f1 m3f3          2        3 0.4814452       TRUE      TRUE #> 14 m1f1 m2f2          1        2 0.4558587       TRUE      TRUE

what: The problem: which factor solution is right?
title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: Anyone applied factor analysis faces dilemma: many factors extract? Parallel analysis might say 5; scree plot might suggest 3; reviewer might insist 1. solution seems describe data differently, tempting treat competing answers question. Goldberg (2006) reframed problem: different solutions competing вАФ complementary. 1-factor solution captures broadest shared variance data, dimension along variables correlate. 5-factor solution captures narrower, specific dimensions. two levels resolution describe underlying structure different vantage points, just satellite image street map describe city. bass-ackwards method makes relationship explicit. fits factor models every level 1 k, computes correlations factor scores across adjacent levels. correlations show : narrow factor inherits broad factor вАФ lineage dimension move coarse fine resolution. factor splits вАФ single broad factor correlates strongly two narrower factors fragmenting sub-dimensions. factor stable вАФ factor correlates ~1.0 counterpart adjacent level essentially construct appearing two resolutions. result hierarchy map вАФ visual numerical account factor structure data builds broad narrow. Note: descriptive, data-driven hierarchy, confirmatory hierarchical model like SchmidвАУLeiman higher-order SEM. -level correlations score correlations (algebraic equivalents), model parameters.

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: use bfi25, built-25-item Big Five example dataset. items measure five personality traits вАФ Agreeableness (A1вАУA5), Conscientiousness (C1вАУC5), Extraversion (E1вАУE5), Neuroticism (N1вАУN5), Openness (O1вАУO5) вАФ 6-point Likert scale. See ?bfi25 full provenance; data derive SAPA project using public-domain IPIP items. drop cases missing item. real analysis might use pairwise deletion multiple imputation; na.omit() sufficient illustration.
code: library(ackwards)  bfi <- na.omit(bfi25) dim(bfi) #> [1] 875  25

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: fitting hierarchy, get sense plausible range k. suggest_k() runs five complementary selection criteria reports consensus range:  suggest_k() return single вАЬcorrectвАЭ k вАФ reports several criteria recommend, can read plausible range rather point estimate. range informs k_max ackwards(), upper bound hierarchy depth, claim true number factors: setting k_max one two levels consensus watch factors fragment intentional informative. full explanation criterion, bias direction, match engine, see vignette("ackwards-suggest-k").
code: sk <- suggest_k(bfi, seed = 42) #> вДє Running parallel analysis (20 iterations, PC + FA)... #> вЬФ Running parallel analysis (20 iterations, PC + FA)... [304ms] #>  #> вДє Running MAP and VSS... #> вЬФ Running MAP and VSS... [188ms] #>  #> вДє Running Comparison Data (CD)... #> вЬФ Running Comparison Data (CD)... [10.6s] #>  print(sk) #>  #> вФАвФА Factor / Component Count Suggestion (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Variables: 25 #> n: 875 #> Basis: pearson #> Tested k: 1-8 #>  #> вФАвФА Criteria (k = 1-8) вФАвФА #>  #> k = 1: PA-PC вЬФ PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0254 VSS-1 0.5178 VSS-2 0.0000 CD вЬФ #> k = 2: PA-PC вЬФ PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0194 VSS-1 0.5839 VSS-2 0.6719 CD вЬФ #> k = 3: PA-PC вЬФ PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0175 VSS-1 0.5913 VSS-2 0.7354 CD вЬФ #> k = 4: PA-PC вЬФ PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0164 VSS-1 0.6215* VSS-2 0.7837 CD вЬФ #> k = 5: PA-PC вЬФ PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0160* VSS-1 0.5738 VSS-2 0.7950* CD вЬФ #> k = 6: PA-PC - PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0172 VSS-1 0.5594 VSS-2 0.7629 CD вЬФ* #> k = 7: PA-PC - PA-FA - MAP 0.0205 VSS-1 0.5613 VSS-2 0.7616 CD - #> k = 8: PA-PC - PA-FA - MAP 0.0236 VSS-1 0.5600 VSS-2 0.7215 CD - #>  #> вФАвФА Recommendations вФАвФА #>  #> вАҐ PA-PC: k <= 5 #> вАҐ PA-FA: k <= 6 #> вАҐ MAP: k = 5 #> вАҐ VSS-1: k = 4 #> вАҐ VSS-2: k = 5 #> вАҐ CD: k = 6 #> Consensus range: k = 4-6 #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: k_max in ackwards() is a maximum depth. Setting k_max one or two levels #> above the consensus to observe factor fragmentation is intentional. #> Caution: PA-PC tends to overextract; structures may not replicate (Forbes, #> 2023). PA-FA and CD are more conservative. Use the range. autoplot(sk)

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: Now fit bass-ackwards hierarchy. important arguments : BFI items ordinal, use cor = "polychoric". computes polychoric correlations items factor extraction, giving accurate representation latent structure. warning time вАФ specifying cor = "polychoric" tells ackwards() already thought measurement scale. varimax? Within level, ackwards() rotates factors orthogonally using varimax, merely cosmetic default. Orthogonality gives -level score correlations closed form: orthonormal rotation satisfies TвА≤ = TвБї¬є, exactly makes WвА≤RW edge algebra exact rather approximation. Varimax orthogonal rotation Goldberg (2006) used, rotation вАЬCF-VARIMAXвАЭ reported Mplus-based papers Kim & Eaton (2015) вАФ CF(ќЇ = 1/p) varimax вАФ two labels competing choices. Oblique rotations deliberately offered: correlated factors confound cross-level signal method exists measure.
code: x <- ackwards(bfi, k_max = 5, cor = "polychoric")

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: print() gives quick overview: вАЬLevelsвАЭ section confirms five models converged, reports cumulative variance explained level. Notice jump k = 1 k = 2 large (23.2% вЖТ 35.5%), later jumps smaller вАФ characteristic data strong general factor several specific dimensions. вАЬEdgesвАЭ section reports many 40 possible -level connections exceed display threshold |r| вЙ• 0.3. 40 comes summing across adjacent pairs: 1√Ч2 + 2√Ч3 + 3√Ч4 + 4√Ч5 = 2 + 6 + 12 + 20 = 40. summary() gives detailed view вАФ per-factor variance fit indices level, plus lineage list showing factors level descend parents: glance() returns top-level information one-row data frame, convenient comparisons across models:
code: print(x) #>  #> вФАвФА Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: polychoric #> n: 875 #> k (max): 5 #>  #> вФАвФА Levels вФАвФА #>  #> вЬФ k = 1: 1 factor, 23.2% variance #> вЬФ k = 2: 2 factors, 35.5% variance #> вЬФ k = 3: 3 factors, 44.6% variance #> вЬФ k = 4: 4 factors, 52.2% variance #> вЬФ k = 5: 5 factors, 58.4% variance #>  #> вФАвФА Edges вФАвФА #>  #> 14 of 40 edges have |r| вЙ• 0.3 #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. Per-level fit indices #> (EFA/ESEM) describe how well a k-factor model fits the items at that level -- #> they do not validate the edges or the hierarchy itself. summary(x) #>  #> вФАвФА Summary: Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: polychoric #> n: 875 #> k (max): 5 #>  #> вФАвФА Levels вФАвФА #>  #> k = 1: 1 factor (23.2% cumulative variance) #> m1f1 23.21% eigenvalue 5.8 #> k = 2: 2 factors (35.5% cumulative variance) #> m2f1 20.94% eigenvalue 5.8 #> m2f2 14.54% eigenvalue 3.07 #> k = 3: 3 factors (44.6% cumulative variance) #> m3f1 18.04% eigenvalue 5.8 #> m3f2 13.89% eigenvalue 3.07 #> m3f3 12.66% eigenvalue 2.28 #> k = 4: 4 factors (52.2% cumulative variance) #> m4f1 17.5% eigenvalue 5.8 #> m4f2 13.63% eigenvalue 3.07 #> m4f3 11.83% eigenvalue 2.28 #> m4f4 9.21% eigenvalue 1.9 #> k = 5: 5 factors (58.4% cumulative variance) #> m5f1 13.75% eigenvalue 5.8 #> m5f2 13.56% eigenvalue 3.07 #> m5f3 11.9% eigenvalue 2.28 #> m5f4 10.09% eigenvalue 1.9 #> m5f5 9.12% eigenvalue 1.56 #>  #> вФАвФА Lineage (primary parents) вФАвФА #>  #> m1f1 вЖТ m2f1, m2f2 #> m2f1 вЖТ m3f1, m3f3 #> m2f2 вЖТ m3f2 #> m3f1 вЖТ m4f1 #> m3f2 вЖТ m4f2 #> m3f3 вЖТ m4f3, m4f4 #> m4f1 вЖТ m5f1, m5f4 #> m4f2 вЖТ m5f2 #> m4f3 вЖТ m5f3 #> m4f4 вЖТ m5f5 #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. Per-level fit indices #> (EFA/ESEM) describe how well a k-factor model fits the items at that level -- #> they do not validate the edges or the hierarchy itself. glance(x) #>   engine rotation        cor k_max n_obs deepest_converged n_edges CFI TLI #> 1    pca  varimax polychoric     5   875                 5      40  NA  NA #>   RMSEA SRMR BIC #> 1    NA   NA  NA

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: hierarchy diagram centerpiece method. row represents one level (k = 1 top, k = 5 bottom). Arrows connect factor primary parent вАФ factor level strongest correlation (|r|). Arrow thickness proportional |r|, color shows direction (blue = positive, red = negative default); aesthetics come legend. clean solution every drawn edge positive, blue appears . left--right layout (handy wide slides), pass direction = "horizontal".  Reading diagram broad (top) narrow (bottom) tells story Big Five: k = 1: One broad factor. Given polychoric basis, captures shared variance across 25 items. k = 2: broad factor splits two: Neuroticism separates mixed cluster Extraversion, Agreeableness, Conscientiousness, Openness items (Extraversion items load strongly within cluster). k = 3: non-Neuroticism cluster splits , separating Extraversion/Agreeableness Conscientiousness/Openness; Neuroticism stays intact sheds residual cross-loadings. k = 4: Conscientiousness Openness differentiate factors. k = 5: Extraversion Agreeableness finally differentiate, completing canonical Big Five. factors labeled m{k}f{j} (level k, factor j). stable IDs used throughout object, m5f1 k = 5 refers factor loadings, edge table, factor scores, diagram.
code: autoplot(x)

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis

Reading hierarchy factors giving names topic вАФ including sign convention, naming across levels, applying labels diagram. See vignette("ackwards-interpret") full workflow.
code: top_items(x, level = 5, cut = 0.5) #>  #> вФАвФА Salient items by factor (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Engine: pca #> Cut: |loading| >= 0.5 #> Top-n: all #>  #> вФАвФА Level 5 (5 factors) вФАвФА #>  #> m5f1 #> E2 [-0.752] #> E4 [0.747] #> E1 [-0.701] #> E3 [0.677] #> E5 [0.597] #> m5f2 #> N3 [-0.825] #> N1 [-0.810] #> N2 [-0.805] #> N5 [-0.688] #> N4 [-0.646] #> m5f3 #> C2 [0.735] #> C4 [-0.716] #> C1 [0.690] #> C3 [0.679] #> C5 [-0.652] #> m5f4 #> A1 [-0.704] #> A3 [0.703] #> A2 [0.692] #> A5 [0.580] #> A4 [0.522] #> m5f5 #> O5 [-0.705] #> O3 [0.655] #> O1 [0.604] #> O2 [-0.595] #> O4 [0.551] #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Loadings reflect primary-parent sign alignment. Use tidy(x, what = "loadings") #> for the full matrix.

previous_headings: Step 5: Dig into the factors tidy()
title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: edge -level factor-score correlation computed via WallerвАЩs (2007) closed-form WвА≤RW algebra вАФ exact result requires score materialization, just weight matrices input correlation matrix. primary_only = TRUE keeps just strongest-connecting edge factor вАФ primary parent вАФ sort = "strength" orders |r|. r values close 1.0 indicate factors nearly identical across adjacent levels вАФ sign stable, replicable dimension. Smaller values indicate structure reorganizing.
code: # Each factor's primary-parent edge, strongest first tidy(x, what = "edges", primary_only = TRUE, sort = "strength") #>    from   to level_from level_to         r is_primary above_cut #> 1  m4f2 m5f2          4        5 0.9984791       TRUE      TRUE #> 2  m3f1 m4f1          3        4 0.9938371       TRUE      TRUE #> 3  m4f4 m5f5          4        5 0.9894063       TRUE      TRUE #> 4  m2f2 m3f2          2        3 0.9873651       TRUE      TRUE #> 5  m4f3 m5f3          4        5 0.9824895       TRUE      TRUE #> 6  m3f2 m4f2          3        4 0.9761484       TRUE      TRUE #> 7  m1f1 m2f1          1        2 0.8900522       TRUE      TRUE #> 8  m2f1 m3f1          2        3 0.8740850       TRUE      TRUE #> 9  m4f1 m5f1          4        5 0.8377659       TRUE      TRUE #> 10 m3f3 m4f3          3        4 0.7316162       TRUE      TRUE #> 11 m3f3 m4f4          3        4 0.6802343       TRUE      TRUE #> 12 m4f1 m5f4          4        5 0.5458269       TRUE      TRUE #> 13 m2f1 m3f3          2        3 0.4814452       TRUE      TRUE #> 14 m1f1 m2f2          1        2 0.4558587       TRUE      TRUE

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: Factor scores place every observation factor every level. useful regression, clustering, downstream analysis want continuous summary latent dimension. augment(x, data = bfi) computes scores fly stored weight matrices. default appends data frame; pass append = FALSE get just score columns, named .m{k}f{j}. Row order preserved either way, scores line input rows. Scores standardized model-implied variance 1 вАФ meaning scaling comes polychoric correlation matrix, raw data. practice empirical standard deviations close exactly 1 non-Pearson basis used; see ?augment.ackwards details. pure PCA Pearson correlations model-implied empirical variances agree exactly.
code: scores <- augment(x, data = bfi, append = FALSE) #> Warning: ! Factor scores are standardized using model-implied SDs from a "polychoric" #>   correlation matrix. #> вДє The raw projection uses `.standardize(data)` (Pearson z-scores), but #>   `score_var` comes from the "polychoric" R. #> вДє Empirical score SDs will differ from 1.0. For non-Pearson analyses, #>   between-level edges from `tidy()` are the authoritative associations. #> This warning is displayed once per session. dim(scores) # 15 score columns (1+2+3+4+5) #> [1] 875  15 names(scores) #>  [1] ".m1f1" ".m2f1" ".m2f2" ".m3f1" ".m3f2" ".m3f3" ".m4f1" ".m4f2" ".m4f3" #> [10] ".m4f4" ".m5f1" ".m5f2" ".m5f3" ".m5f4" ".m5f5"

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: augment() returns plain data frame, scores slot directly standard R workflow. quick illustration, k = 5 factor scores nearly uncorrelated вАФ consequence orthogonal rotation вАФ scores across levels highly correlated along primary-parent lineage: cross-level block confirms lineage: .m4f1 correlates strongly .m5f1 .m5f4 (k = 5 factors spawned) near-zero .m5f2 (descends different k = 4 factor). sanity check can run result вАФ strong parentвАУchild correlations appear exactly tidy(= "edges") table says .

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: scoring needs stored weight matrices fit-time item means/SDs, can apply fitted hierarchy observations model never saw вАФ standard cross-validation pattern fitting training split scoring held-test split without retraining. predict() front door (augment(x, data = ..., append = FALSE) equivalent): test observations standardized training moments (default scaling = "fit"), personвАЩs score depend else landed test split, train test scores share one metric вАФ compare like like across splits, feed downstream model, pool : See ?predict.ackwards Scoring new observations section ?augment.ackwards full semantics (including scaling = "sample" deliberately re-standardizing new population).
code: set.seed(11) idx <- sample(nrow(bfi), 500) x_train <- ackwards(bfi[idx, ], k_max = 5, cor = "polychoric")  test_scores <- predict(x_train, bfi[-idx, ]) head(round(test_scores[, 1:5], 2)) #>   .m1f1 .m2f1 .m2f2 .m3f1 .m3f2 #> 1 -1.25 -1.18 -0.42 -1.21 -0.37 #> 2  1.23  0.56  1.68  0.63  1.65 #> 3 -0.93 -1.37  0.69 -1.17  0.78 #> 4  0.05 -0.16  0.44  0.01  0.47 #> 5 -0.62 -0.33 -0.76 -0.23 -0.72 #> 6 -0.17 -0.44  0.54  0.01  0.63 train_scores <- predict(x_train, bfi[idx, ]) # Same construct, same scale: pooled scores line up with the fitted solution round(colMeans(train_scores[, 1:3]), 3) # ~0 by construction (training data) #> .m1f1 .m2f1 .m2f2  #>     0     0     0 round(colMeans(test_scores[, 1:3]), 3) # near 0: test split scored on the same metric #>  .m1f1  .m2f1  .m2f2  #> -0.046 -0.043 -0.018

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: bass-ackwards workflow ackwards six steps: suggest_k(data) вАФ identify plausible range hierarchy depth. ackwards(data, k_max, cor = ...) вАФ fit full hierarchy factor models. print(x) / summary(x) / glance(x) вАФ check convergence, read per-level variance fit indices, inspect lineage list. autoplot(x) вАФ visualize hierarchy lineage diagram. tidy(x, = ...) вАФ extract loadings, edges, variance form ready tables analysis. augment(x, data = ...) вАФ generate factor scores downstream use.

title: Introduction to Bass-Ackwards Analysis
text: Goldberg, L. R. (2006). bass-ackwards: development hierarchical factor structures top . Journal Research Personality, 40(4), 347вАУ358. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jrp.2006.01.001 Horn, J. L. (1965). rationale test number factors factor analysis. Psychometrika, 30(2), 179вАУ185. Velicer, W. F. (1976). Determining number components matrix partial correlations. Psychometrika, 41(3), 321вАУ327. Waller, N. G. (2007). general method computing hierarchical component structures Bass-Ackward factor analysis. Journal Research Personality, 41(4), 745вАУ752. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jrp.2006.08.005

what: Why Pearson underestimates for ordinal items
title: Ordinal Data: Polychoric Correlations and WLSMV
text: continuous latent trait sliced small number ordered categories, observed correlation two items always lower correlation underlying latent variables. coarse rating scale, larger attenuation. 5-point scale, Pearson correlations can 10вАУ20% lower true latent correlations; 2-point (binary) scale, underestimate can exceed 30%. matters bass-ackwards analysis : Loadings attenuated. Factors appear weaker . -level edges attenuated. hierarchy looks flatter вАФ factors nearly perfectly correlated across levels may appear moderately correlated. number factors underestimated. Attenuation compresses eigenvalue spectrum, selection criteria (parallel analysis, MAP) may suggest fewer factors truly exist.

title: Ordinal Data: Polychoric Correlations and WLSMV
text: polychoric correlation two ordinal items estimates Pearson correlation exist underlying continuous latent variables, variables measured without discretization. fitting bivariate normal model thresholds category boundary. binary (two-category) items estimate traditionally called tetrachoric correlation, separate method вАФ tetrachoric simply two-category special case polychoric. cor = "polychoric" handles binary items automatically: psych::polychoric() detects two-level case returns tetrachoric estimate, never request tetrachoric separately. Setting cor = "polychoric" ackwards() replaces step 1 pipeline вАФ computing item correlation matrix R вАФ polychoric matrix. downstream computation (factor extraction, rotation, tenBerge scoring, -level algebra) operates polychoric R.

title: Ordinal Data: Polychoric Correlations and WLSMV

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: suggest_k() computes five criteria data. logic, typical behavior, practical limitations.

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: . Computes PC eigenvalues observed correlation matrix, simulates large number random correlation matrices dimensions. Retains components whose observed eigenvalues exceed 95th percentile simulated distribution. Typical behavior. Tends overextract вАФ often recommends components replicate independent samples, particularly items moderately correlated (usually personality clinical research). Treat recommendation upper bound. useful. Best paired engine = "pca" ackwards(), since operate PC eigenvalue basis. also provides fast, stable baseline across wide range data types. Limitation. 95th-percentile threshold convention, formal test. large samples, even trivial components can exceed threshold.

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: . logic PA-PC, applied common-factor eigenvalues rather PC eigenvalues. observed FA eigenvalues compared 95th percentile simulated FA eigenvalues. Typical behavior. conservative PA-PC. FA eigenvalues smaller (exclude unique variance), fewer exceed random-data threshold. PA-FA recommendations typically equal fall PA-PC. useful. model-consistent criterion engine = "efa" engine = "esem" ackwards(): analysis assumes common-factor model, comparing FA eigenvalues FA baseline better-matched test. Limitation. Like PA-PC, can underpowered small samples returns NA observed FA eigenvalue exceeds random threshold (sign data may support factor model ).

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?

Simulation studies find performs well across range sample sizes factor structures, particularly true number factors small moderate. useful. reliable secondary check catches cases PA overextracts. MAP PA-FA agree, can fairly confident range. Limitation. MAP operates PC components internally (uses psych::vss() fm = "pc"), recommendation reflects component-extraction framework even plan use EFA ESEM engine ackwards().

title: Choosing k: How Many Factors?
text: . Generates comparison datasets drawing marginal distributions observed items (preserving itemвАЩs shape without assuming multivariate normality). Computes eigenvalues comparison dataset applies sequential one-sided Wilcoxon test (default ќ± = 0.30): factor retained adding significantly reduces RMSE relative previous level; procedure stops first non-significant improvement. recommended k last retained level вАФ necessarily minimum RMSE curve. Typical behavior. Among accurate criteria simulation studies (Ruscio & Roche, 2012), particularly items non-normal distributions вАФ common feature Likert-scale data. conservative PA-PC. useful. Ordinal skewed data normality assumptions underlying PA questionable. CD samples actual marginal distributions, implicitly captures item skewness discreteness. Limitation. Requires EFAtools package (install separately). Needs raw data matrix вАФ run correlation matrix alone. resampling step stochastic, results can vary slightly across runs (use seed reproducibility). suggest_k() reports cd_available = FALSE EFAtools absent skips CD gracefully. note cor = "spearman": four criteria respect cor argument compute eigenvalues requested correlation matrix. text: output prints two sections. criteria table shows raw evidence k, using two display conventions: Checkmark / dash (PA-PC, PA-FA, CD): checkmark (вЬФ) marks k criterion retains, recommended ceiling; dash (-) marks levels ceiling, retain. criteria answer yes/вАЬkeep level?вАЭ question k, number show. Number star (MAP, VSS-1, VSS-2): criteria produce score k вАФ quantity minimize (MAP) maximize (VSS) вАФ raw value printed, single optimal k (min max) starred (*). Reading surrounding numbers tells sharp optimum : lone tall peak decisive, near-flat plateau . recommendations block summarizes criterionвАЩs single suggested k (range PA), consensus range spans minimum maximum across available recommendations.
