Determining number factors retain exploratory factor analysis using comparison data known factorial structure. Psychological Assessment, 24(2), 282вАУ292. https://doi.org/10.1037/a0025697 Velicer, W. F. (1976). Determining number components matrix partial correlations. Psychometrika, 41(3), 321вАУ327. https://doi.org/10.1007/BF02293557

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: default diagram reference:  Factors labeled m{k}f{j} (level k, factor j). Two edge aesthetics carry -level correlations, one comes legend: arrow thickness encodes magnitude |r|, edge color encodes direction (blue = positive, redвАУorange = negative). Level labels left count factors per level. Primary-parent edges always positive sign alignment; red (negative) edge therefore genuine secondary relationship, artifact.
code: library(ackwards)  bfi <- na.omit(bfi25) x <- ackwards(bfi, k_max = 5, cor = "polychoric") autoplot(x)

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: choose aesthetic carries piece information. sign_by picks channel direction magnitude_by picks channel |r|. aesthetic ever mapped without matching legend.

what: sign_by вАФ how direction is shown
title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: sign_by = "color" (default) uses color_pos/color_neg. "linetype" draws positive edges solid negative edges dashed, freeing color single-hue figure. "" uses color linetype together вАФ negatives get distinct double-dash still read greyscale вАФ merges two single вАЬDirectionвАЭ legend. "none" drops sign encoding entirely.
code: autoplot(x, sign_by = "linetype") autoplot(x, sign_by = "both")

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: default magnitude_by = "linewidth" maps |r| arrow thickness |r| legend. Set magnitude_by = "none" uniform-width edges (see also edge_linewidth, , pin specific width).
code: autoplot(x, magnitude_by = "none")

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: Edges cut_show hidden entirely. Raising produces sparser diagram emphasises strongest connections.
code: # Default cut_show = 0.3 (already shown above) autoplot(x, cut_show = 0.5)

what: color_pos / color_neg вАФ custom direction colors
title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: default blue/red palette can replaced colors recognised R. British spellings (colour_pos, colour_neg) accepted aliases.  sign encoded color (sign_by = "linetype" "none"), edges take single color_edge (default black) вАФ basis Forbes (2023) publication style (see worked example end vignette).
code: autoplot(x, color_pos = "darkorchid", color_neg = "darkorange")

what: mono = TRUE вАФ a black-and-white convenience wrapper
title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: mono = TRUE shorthand sign_by = "linetype" black edges: solid lines positive correlations, dashed lines negative. magnitude_by still applies, linewidth continues encode |r|.  mono suits black--white figures reader must distinguish positive negative edges. label edges exact values well, add show_r = TRUE (documented next).
code: autoplot(x, mono = TRUE)

what: show_r / r_digits вАФ annotate edges with r values
title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: show_r = TRUE draws rounded (signed) correlation edge midpoint. r_digits controls number decimal places (default 2).
code: autoplot(x, show_r = TRUE) autoplot(x, show_r = TRUE, r_digits = 1)

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: node_labels named character vector mapping factor IDs display strings. Unspecified factors keep m{k}f{j} labels.  Multi-line labels supported via \n:
code: autoplot(x, node_labels = c(   m5f1 = "Neuro.",   m5f2 = "Extra.",   m5f3 = "Consc.",   m5f4 = "Agree.",   m5f5 = "Open." )) autoplot(x, node_labels = c(   m5f1 = "Neuro-\nticism",   m5f2 = "Extra-\nversion" ))

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: Typing every factor ID tedious large objects. label_template() generates full named vector canonical diagram order prints copy-pasteable c(...) literal can edit pass back node_labels. also offers Forbes (2023) letter convention ("A1", "B1", "B2", вА¶) built-style:  full naming workflow вАФ reading factors, sign convention, choosing labels across hierarchy вАФ see vignette("ackwards-interpret").

what: primary_only = TRUE вАФ show only primary-parent edges
title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: Setting primary_only = TRUE keeps single strongest edge per factor (primary parent), producing clean tree. skip-level edges never primary, also suppresses curved arcs pairs = "" used.
code: autoplot(x, primary_only = TRUE)

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: Level labels (вАЬ1 factorвАЭ, вАЬ2 factorsвАЭ, вА¶) shown default left margin. can hidden resized.
code: autoplot(x, show_level_labels = FALSE) autoplot(x, show_level_labels = TRUE, level_label_size = 4)

what: show_arrows = FALSE вАФ plain line ends
title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: default, edges end closed arrowheads. Setting show_arrows = FALSE draws plain line ends. applies straight edges curved skip-level arcs.
code: autoplot(x, show_arrows = FALSE)

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: default, edge width proportional |r| (via magnitude_by). numeric edge_linewidth draws every edge constant width removes |r| legend вАФ like magnitude_by = "none", width choose.
code: autoplot(x, edge_linewidth = 0.7)

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: default levels stack top--bottom (level 1 top). direction = "horizontal" lays left--right (level 1 left), fits wide slides posters; level labels move bottom margin.
code: autoplot(x, direction = "horizontal")

what: legend = FALSE вАФ suppress all guides
title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: legend = FALSE removes legends plot. useful diagram self-explanatory legend duplicates information conveyed labels.
code: autoplot(x, legend = FALSE)

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: following call reproduces visual style Forbes (2023): black lines uniform weight, plain line ends, correlation labels, legend. Setting direction colors black yields single-hue figure, legend = FALSE suppresses now-redundant key.  Combining node_labels names factors final figure:  pruned-factor variant figure (nodes omitted, spanning arrows) see vignette("ackwards-forbes").
code: autoplot(x,   color_pos      = "black",   color_neg      = "black",   edge_linewidth = 0.6,   show_arrows    = FALSE,   show_r         = TRUE,   legend         = FALSE ) autoplot(x,   color_pos = "black",   color_neg = "black",   edge_linewidth = 0.6,   show_arrows = FALSE,   show_r = TRUE,   legend = FALSE,   node_labels = c(     m5f1 = "Neuro.",     m5f2 = "Extra.",     m5f3 = "Consc.",     m5f4 = "Agree.",     m5f5 = "Open."   ) )

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: autoplot() returns ordinary ggplot object, save ggplot2::ggsave(): ackwards re-export ggsave() вАФ move ggplot2 Suggests Imports вАФ call ggplot2 directly.
code: p <- autoplot(x, direction = "horizontal") ggplot2::ggsave("hierarchy.png", p, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram
text: suggest_k() also autoplot() method, producing multi-panel scree / parallel-analysis / VSS diagnostic. documented depth vignette("ackwards-suggest-k"); note autoplot() generic covers .
code: sk <- suggest_k(bfi, seed = 42) #> вДє Running parallel analysis (20 iterations, PC + FA)... #> вЬФ Running parallel analysis (20 iterations, PC + FA)... [284ms] #>  #> вДє Running MAP and VSS... #> вЬФ Running MAP and VSS... [179ms] #>  #> вДє Running Comparison Data (CD)... #> вЬФ Running Comparison Data (CD)... [11.6s] #>  autoplot(sk)

title: Customizing the Hierarchy Diagram

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis R ackwards implements GoldbergвАЩs (2006) bass-ackwards method modern extensions mapping hierarchical structure multivariate data. core insight simple: instead asking many factors best describe data, ask solutions different levels resolution related one another. 1-factor solution captures broadest shared variance; 5-factor solution captures narrower, specific dimensions. Bass-ackwards analysis traces broad factors split narrow ones вАФ narrow factors redundant re-combinations broader ones. package supports three extraction engines (PCA, EFA, ESEM), polychoric correlations ordinal data, ForbesвАЩs (2023) extension reveals skip-level connections flags redundant artifactual factors.

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("jmgirard/ackwards")

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: use bfi25, built-25-item Big Five example dataset (see ?bfi25 provenance). BFI items recorded 6-point ordinal scale, set cor = "polychoric".

what: Step 1 вАФ Suggest a range of k
title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: suggest_k() runs five complementary criteria вАФ two forms parallel analysis (PC FA basis), MAP, VSS, optionally Comparison Data вАФ help choose upper bound hierarchy depth. criteria converge consensus range covers k = 5, consistent known Big Five structure instrument.
code: library(ackwards)  bfi <- na.omit(bfi25) sk <- suggest_k(bfi) #> вДє Running parallel analysis (20 iterations, PC + FA)... #> вЬФ Running parallel analysis (20 iterations, PC + FA)... [178ms] #>  #> вДє Running MAP and VSS... #> вЬФ Running MAP and VSS... [98ms] #>  #> вДє Running Comparison Data (CD)... #> вЬФ Running Comparison Data (CD)... [4.6s] #>  sk #>  #> вФАвФА Factor / Component Count Suggestion (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Variables: 25 #> n: 875 #> Basis: pearson #> Tested k: 1-8 #>  #> вФАвФА Criteria (k = 1-8) вФАвФА #>  #> k = 1: PA-PC вЬФ PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0254 VSS-1 0.5178 VSS-2 0.0000 CD вЬФ #> k = 2: PA-PC вЬФ PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0194 VSS-1 0.5839 VSS-2 0.6719 CD вЬФ #> k = 3: PA-PC вЬФ PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0175 VSS-1 0.5913 VSS-2 0.7354 CD вЬФ #> k = 4: PA-PC вЬФ PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0164 VSS-1 0.6215* VSS-2 0.7837 CD вЬФ #> k = 5: PA-PC вЬФ PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0160* VSS-1 0.5738 VSS-2 0.7950* CD вЬФ #> k = 6: PA-PC - PA-FA вЬФ MAP 0.0172 VSS-1 0.5594 VSS-2 0.7629 CD вЬФ* #> k = 7: PA-PC - PA-FA - MAP 0.0205 VSS-1 0.5613 VSS-2 0.7616 CD - #> k = 8: PA-PC - PA-FA - MAP 0.0236 VSS-1 0.5600 VSS-2 0.7215 CD - #>  #> вФАвФА Recommendations вФАвФА #>  #> вАҐ PA-PC: k <= 5 #> вАҐ PA-FA: k <= 6 #> вАҐ MAP: k = 5 #> вАҐ VSS-1: k = 4 #> вАҐ VSS-2: k = 5 #> вАҐ CD: k = 6 #> Consensus range: k = 4-6 #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: k_max in ackwards() is a maximum depth. Setting k_max one or two levels #> above the consensus to observe factor fragmentation is intentional. #> Caution: PA-PC tends to overextract; structures may not replicate (Forbes, #> 2023). PA-FA and CD are more conservative. Use the range.

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: ackwards() fits factor models every level 1 k_max computes -level factor-score correlations define hierarchy.
code: x <- ackwards(bfi, k_max = 5, cor = "polychoric") x #>  #> вФАвФА Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: polychoric #> n: 875 #> k (max): 5 #>  #> вФАвФА Levels вФАвФА #>  #> вЬФ k = 1: 1 factor, 23.2% variance #> вЬФ k = 2: 2 factors, 35.5% variance #> вЬФ k = 3: 3 factors, 44.6% variance #> вЬФ k = 4: 4 factors, 52.2% variance #> вЬФ k = 5: 5 factors, 58.4% variance #>  #> вФАвФА Edges вФАвФА #>  #> 14 of 40 edges have |r| вЙ• 0.3 #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. Per-level fit indices #> (EFA/ESEM) describe how well a k-factor model fits the items at that level -- #> they do not validate the edges or the hierarchy itself.

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: autoplot() draws hierarchical diagram. row level (k = 1 top, k = 5 bottom); arrows show narrow factors inherit broad factor. Arrow thickness encodes |r| arrow color encodes direction (blue = positive, red = negative), legend вАФ clean solution happens negative edges, blue appears . Pass direction = "horizontal" left--right layout. visualization vignette covers sign encoding rest styling options.  five-factor level cleanly splits Big Five. single broad factor k = 1 (roughly general positive character) differentiates first positive vs.¬†negative affect (k = 2), successively narrower traits.
code: autoplot(x)

title: Bass-Ackwards Hierarchical Structural Analysis
text: tidy() extracts part object tidy data frame; augment() appends factor scores data downstream analysis.
code: # Eight strongest adjacent-level primary edges edges <- tidy(x, what = "edges", sort = "strength") head(edges[edges$is_primary, c("from", "to", "r")], 8) #>   from   to         r #> 1 m4f2 m5f2 0.9984791 #> 2 m3f1 m4f1 0.9938371 #> 3 m4f4 m5f5 0.9894063 #> 4 m2f2 m3f2 0.9873651 #> 5 m4f3 m5f3 0.9824895 #> 6 m3f2 m4f2 0.9761484 #> 7 m1f1 m2f1 0.8900522 #> 8 m2f1 m3f1 0.8740850 # Append scores for all 5 levels to the original data frame scored <- augment(x, data = bfi) #> Warning: ! Factor scores are standardized using model-implied SDs from a "polychoric" #>   correlation matrix. #> вДє The raw projection uses `.standardize(data)` (Pearson z-scores), but #>   `score_var` comes from the "polychoric" R. #> вДє Empirical score SDs will differ from 1.0. For non-Pearson analyses, #>   between-level edges from `tidy()` are the authoritative associations. #> This warning is displayed once per session. dim(scored) # original 25 items + 1+2+3+4+5 = 15 score columns #> [1] 875  40  # The appended score columns are named .m{k}f{j} grep("^\\.m", names(scored), value = TRUE) #>  [1] ".m1f1" ".m2f1" ".m2f2" ".m3f1" ".m3f2" ".m3f3" ".m4f1" ".m4f2" ".m4f3" #> [10] ".m4f4" ".m5f1" ".m5f2" ".m5f3" ".m5f4" ".m5f5"

what: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis вАФ ackwards
title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis вАФ ackwards
text: Extracts factor/component solutions levels 1 k, characterises hierarchy computing -level factor-score correlations. "hierarchy" descriptive: edges score correlations, fitted higher-order SEM.

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis вАФ ackwards
code: ackwards(   data,   k_max,   engine = "pca",   cor = "pearson",   fm = "minres",   estimator = NULL,   missing = "pairwise",   n_obs = NULL,   align_signs = TRUE,   keep_scores = FALSE,   keep_fits = FALSE,   seed = NULL,   pairs = "adjacent",   cut_show = 0.3,   ... )

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis вАФ ackwards
text: data data frame numeric matrix observed variables (items columns, observations rows). Alternatively, pre-computed correlation matrix may supplied (square, symmetric, numeric matrix unit diagonal). correlation matrix supplied, engine must "pca" "efa" (ESEM requires raw data), missing cor arguments ignored. See Correlation-matrix input section . k_max Maximum number factors/components extract. Required; use suggest_k() uncertain. Sets depth hierarchy: levels 1 k_max extracted retained. (Note: suggest_k()'s k_max means something related distinct вАУ max number factors/components evaluates recommending depth, depth ; see function's docs.) engine Extraction engine: "pca" (default), "efa", "esem". "esem" uses lavaan::efa() rotation-aware SEs per-level fit indices; recommended clinical/HiTOP workflow (Kim & Eaton, 2015; Forbush et al., 2024). Requires lavaan >= 0.6-13. cor Correlation basis: "pearson" (default), "spearman", "polychoric". PCA/EFA, "polychoric" computes polychoric correlation matrix via psych::polychoric() (requires psych). ESEM, triggers WLSMV estimation via lavaan. cli warning emitted ordinal-looking columns detected cor "polychoric". fm Factor extraction method passed psych::fa(); used engine = "efa". One "minres" (default, robust OLS), "ml" (maximum likelihood, gives chi-square fit converges less reliably deep levels), "pa" (principal axis). Ignored engine = "pca". estimator Estimation method ESEM engine. NULL (default) auto-selects: "WLSMV" cor = "polychoric", "ML" otherwise. Pass explicitly override: "ULSMV" (unweighted WLS), "MLR" (robust ML). cor = "polychoric" estimator = "ML"/"MLR" errors (lavaan support ML/MLR ordered indicators); "WLSMV"/ "ULSMV" continuous cor allowed (valid, atypical, continuous WLS/ADF estimator). Ignored PCA EFA engines. effective value (auto-selection) recorded x$meta$estimator (NA PCA/EFA). missing handle missing item responses. One : "pairwise" (default) вАУ use available observations pairwise. PCA/EFA feeds stats::cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs"). ESEM WLSMV/ULSMV (ordinal), lavaan uses available.cases, computes polychoric thresholds correlations rows contribute pair вАУ MCAR-valid uses full N. ESEM ML/MLR (continuous), lavaan uses listwise deletion internally edges computed pairwise correlation matrix; minor inconsistency documented $meta. warning emitted incomplete rows detected. "listwise" вАУ complete rows used. Reduces data stats::complete.cases() fitting, correlation matrix, engine fit, edges consistent. n_obs result reflects reduced N. "fiml" вАУ Full Information Maximum Likelihood. engine = "esem" (estimator = "ML"/"MLR"), passes missing = "fiml" lavaan::efa() derives edge correlations lavaan's FIML-estimated saturated model. engine = "pca"/"efa" (M38), correlation matrix estimated via psych::corFiml() fed usual W'RW algebra; requires cor = "pearson" (corFiml estimates multivariate-normal matrix) route announced via cli message. Errors WLSMV/ULSMV non-Pearson PCA/EFA basis. Note: FIML improves estimation missingness impute item responses; score materialisation (keep_scores = TRUE) still produces NA rows incomplete observations. See n_obs fit-index sample size PCA/EFA path. n_obs Number observations, (raw-data FIML path) string selecting N feeds fit indices. Correlation-matrix input: positive integer. Required engine = "efa" (psych::fa() needs N chi-square / RMSEA / TLI); optional "pca" (stored NA_integer_ omitted, disabling N-dependent fit statistics). Raw data: N normally taken nrow(data) numeric n_obs ignored (warning). exception missing = "fiml" engine = "pca"/"efa" (M38): psych::corFiml() estimates correlation matrix incomplete rows, n_obs may "total" (default вАУ every row contributing FIML likelihood, matching FIML convention; Enders, 2010) "complete" (complete-case N, conservative lower bound). Point estimates (loadings, edges) depend choice; EFA fit indices , approximate two-step (FIML matrix normal-theory EFA) route regardless N (Zhang & Savalei, 2020). string n_obs accepted path. align_signs Logical; sign-align factors primary-parent lineage? Default TRUE. keep_scores Logical; store factor scores result? Default FALSE (recomputable via augment.ackwards()). TRUE, per-observation scores stored x$scores named list n x k_j matrices, one per level, standardized real score SDs (see augment.ackwards()). keep_fits Logical; store raw engine fit objects? Default FALSE. TRUE, per-level fit objects (psych lavaan) stored x$fits named list indexed level. seed Integer seed stochastic engines (used PCA captured reproducibility metadata). Default NULL. pairs level pairs compute edges : "adjacent" (default, classic Goldberg вАУ consecutive levels) "" (Forbes extension вАУ every pair levels, including skip-level correlations). prune() recomputes -pairs edges demand regardless setting, pruning require pairs = "" . cut_show Edges |r| >= cut_show flagged above_cut tidy() output. Default 0.3. ... Reserved future arguments.

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis вАФ ackwards
text: object class "ackwards". See print.ackwards(), tidy.ackwards(), glance.ackwards(), augment.ackwards() output methods.

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis вАФ ackwards
text: engine = "pca" вАУ original Goldberg (2006) method; fastest; never fails converge; Waller (2007) algebra exact components. rotation = "varimax" вАУ T'=T^-1 property orthogonal rotation enables closed-form W'RW edge algebra keeps within-level factors uncorrelated cross-level edges reflect hierarchical signal. Matches Goldberg (2006), Kim & Eaton (2015), Forbush et al. (2024). Varimax supported rotation; oblique rotation confound -level signal method's core output. cor = "pearson" вАУ silent basis switching. items look ordinal (<= 7 distinct integer values), cli warning suggest cor = "polychoric", available three engines. align_signs = TRUE вАУ unaligned signs make output unreadable. Anchor: m1f1 oriented toward positive manifold; subsequent factor flipped edge primary parent positive. keep_scores = FALSE / keep_fits = FALSE вАУ memory privacy. Scores O(n x Sigmak) often sensitive; raw engine fits can large. recomputable stored r matrix.

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis вАФ ackwards
text: ESEM engine fits separate lavaan model every level 1..k_max. ordinal data (cor = "polychoric", WLSMV) costly sample statistics lavaan derives raw data вАУ thresholds, polychoric correlation matrix, asymptotic weight matrix вАУ depend data, number factors, computed first level reused every deeper level (identical solutions, much less work). matters many items (hundreds), recomputing statistics level dominated run time. per-level model fits mutually independent dispatched future framework future.apply installed. default plan sequential (behaviour change). run levels parallel, set plan calling ackwards(): Parallelism pays per-level fits heavy (large p, several levels); small problems worker startup cost can outweigh . Results reproducible across plans seed supplied. PCA EFA already compute correlation matrix unaffected.
code: future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 4)  # or multicore on Unix   x <- ackwards(items, k_max = 8, engine = "esem", cor = "polychoric")

title: Bass-ackwards hierarchical structural analysis вАФ ackwards
text: data pre-computed correlation matrix (square, symmetric, unit diagonal), ackwards() runs entirely matrix using W'RW algebra вАУ raw item responses needed. useful published correlation table polychoric matrix computed externally. Constraints behaviour correlation matrix supplied: Engine: "pca" "efa" supported. title: Augment data with factor scores from an ackwards object вАФ augment.ackwards
text: Appends per-observation factor scores every level data frame. Score columns named .m{k}f{j} (e.g., .m1f1, .m2f1, .m2f2), matching factor labels used throughout object.

title: Augment data with factor scores from an ackwards object вАФ augment.ackwards
text: Score computation. Scores S = Z W / sqrt(score_var), Z item z-scores, W per-level weight matrix stored object, sqrt(score_var) standardizes real score standard deviations (Invariant 1: never assume unit variance). PCA method "components"; EFA/ESEM "tenBerge". scaling argument controls means/SDs build Z: default fit-time moments stored object, data score вАФ training data, subset , entirely new observations вАФ lands metric model estimated . Scoring new observations (cross-validation). scoring needs stored weight matrices fit-time moments, can fit ackwards() training split score held-test split without retraining: augment(x, data = test_set) (, equivalently, predict.ackwards()). default scaling = "fit" test observations standardized training means/SDs, "applying trained model" means: test observation's score depend observations happen share split, train test scores directly comparable. scaling = "sample" instead re-standardizes supplied data's moments вАФ deliberate choice scoring sample different population metric, option objects fit correlation matrix (carry raw-data moments). non-Pearson bases (polychoric, Spearman) usual caveat applies either way: weights derive non-Pearson R Z linear standardization, empirical score SDs close exactly 1 (one-time warning says ); train/test comparability scaling = "fit" unaffected. objects fit missing = "fiml" (PCA/EFA), stored moments observed (na.rm) means/SDs training items вАФ correlation matrix FIML-estimated, scoring operates observed responses, observed moments consistent frame; incomplete rows still score NA (scoring impute). Missing data. Score projection applies weights row-wise propagates NAs listwise: observation least one missing item variable produce NA scores every level. differs fitting, uses pairwise-complete correlations. warning issued NA rows detected. Use na.omit(data) scoring NA rows unwanted. Data source. data supplied, scores always recomputed using stored weights вАУ score new observations. data NULL keep_scores = TRUE set fit time, stored scores returned. neither available informative error raised. Scores-output (append = FALSE). default (append = TRUE) scores appended supplied data, following broom convention. Set append = FALSE return score columns вАУ convenient feeding scores straight cor(), lm(), clustering call without dragging item columns along. augment() always preserves row order row count, cbind(data, augment(x, data, append = FALSE)) reproduces appended output exactly. row scores NA (missing item вАУ see Missing data ) still returned place, positional alignment holds even NA scores. rejoin survives filtering scores afterwards, name identifier columns id_cols travel scores.

title: Augment data with factor scores from an ackwards object вАФ augment.ackwards
code: # Score the training data on the fly (no keep_scores = TRUE needed) x <- ackwards(bfi25, k_max = 5) #> Warning: ! 125 rows have missing values; correlations are computed pairwise. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` for consistent complete-case analysis. scores_df <- augment(x, data = bfi25) #> Warning: ! 125 rows contain missing values and will produce NA scores. #> вДє Score projection applies weights row-wise and propagates NAs listwise; FIML #>   estimation does not impute missing item responses. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` when fitting (so the model and scores share the #>   same complete rows), or call `na.omit(data)` before scoring. head(scores_df[, startsWith(names(scores_df), ".m")]) #>         .m1f1      .m2f1      .m2f2       .m3f1      .m3f2       .m3f3 #> 1 -1.30030695 -1.4235382 -0.1052068 -0.85089105  0.2798278 -1.51077483 #> 2  0.01634436  0.5630258 -0.9989376  0.56828756 -0.9942347 -0.08567816 #> 3  0.59542725  0.5404323  0.2526270  0.67694066  0.3150119 -0.10907369 #> 4 -0.71828163 -0.5073496 -0.5700585  0.06274103 -0.2041248 -1.39504136 #> 5 -1.32474768 -1.2750967 -0.4286547 -1.42804149 -0.4773126 -0.05571538 #> 6  0.52117719  0.1647933  0.7867243  0.29379255  0.8458643 -0.07021866 #>        .m4f1      .m4f2       .m4f3       .m4f4       .m5f1        .m5f2 #> 1 -0.7568233  0.2637881 -1.60295498 -0.39893361  0.05797902  0.225048096 #> 2  0.5586824 -1.0007502 -0.03472584  0.09088737  0.43531863 -1.000292190 #> 3  0.6569910  0.4184690 -0.48684202  0.66308866  1.31520127  0.356916365 #> 4  0.1122025 -0.1138340 -1.83242813  0.39875175 -0.70662078 -0.005187861 #> 5 -1.3991091 -0.5747296  0.27674922 -0.71562603 -1.89416329 -0.511204049 #> 6  0.3599258  0.6416350  0.64476695 -1.28366300 -0.16261206  0.664345274 #>         .m5f3      .m5f4       .m5f5 #> 1 -1.41275114 -1.3997979 -0.65487376 #> 2 -0.04555192  0.3557413  0.06686548 #> 3 -0.26674609 -0.6561587  0.31185077 #> 4 -2.12703248  1.2472183  0.85014591 #> 5  0.05521847  0.1890915 -0.33729526 #> 6  0.56488072  0.7357341 -1.16642860  # Scores-only: just the .m{k}f{j} columns, ready for cor()/lm() scores_only <- augment(x, data = bfi25, append = FALSE) #> Warning: ! 125 rows contain missing values and will produce NA scores. #> вДє Score projection applies weights row-wise and propagates NAs listwise; FIML #>   estimation does not impute missing item responses. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` when fitting (so the model and scores share the #>   same complete rows), or call `na.omit(data)` before scoring. round(cor(scores_only[, c(".m5f1", ".m5f2")]), 2) #>       .m5f1 .m5f2 #> .m5f1     1    NA #> .m5f2    NA     1  # Carry an identifier through for a safe post-filter rejoin df <- data.frame(id = seq_len(nrow(bfi25)), bfi25) scored <- augment(x, data = df, append = FALSE, id_cols = "id") #> Warning: ! 125 rows contain missing values and will produce NA scores. #> вДє Score projection applies weights row-wise and propagates NAs listwise; FIML #>   estimation does not impute missing item responses. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` when fitting (so the model and scores share the #>   same complete rows), or call `na.omit(data)` before scoring. head(scored[, c("id", ".m5f1")]) #>   id       .m5f1 #> 1  1  0.05797902 #> 2  2  0.43531863 #> 3  3  1.31520127 #> 4  4 -0.70662078 #> 5  5 -1.89416329 #> 6  6 -0.16261206  # Store at fit time and augment without re-supplying data x2 <- ackwards(bfi25, k_max = 5, keep_scores = TRUE) #> Warning: ! 125 rows have missing values; correlations are computed pairwise. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` for consistent complete-case analysis. #> Warning: ! 125 rows contain missing values and will produce NA scores. #> вДє Score projection applies weights row-wise and propagates NAs listwise; FIML #>   estimation does not impute missing item responses. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` when fitting (so the model and scores share the #>   same complete rows), or call `na.omit(data)` before scoring. scores_df2 <- augment(x2)  # Cross-validation: fit on a training split, score the test split on the # training metric (no retraining; see also predict.ackwards()) train_idx <- seq_len(500) x_train <- ackwards(bfi25[train_idx, ], k_max = 5) #> Warning: ! 58 rows have missing values; correlations are computed pairwise. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` for consistent complete-case analysis. test_scores <- augment(x_train, data = bfi25[-train_idx, ], append = FALSE) #> Warning: ! 67 rows contain missing values and will produce NA scores. #> вДє Score projection applies weights row-wise and propagates NAs listwise; FIML #>   estimation does not impute missing item responses. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` when fitting (so the model and scores share the #>   same complete rows), or call `na.omit(data)` before scoring. head(test_scores) #>         .m1f1      .m2f1      .m2f2      .m3f1      .m3f2     .m3f3      .m4f1 #> 1  1.31327917  1.2430095  0.4608252  0.6680509  0.1248161 1.4375648  0.7088025 #> 2  0.16529258  1.2321417 -1.9494198  1.0643443 -2.0400056 0.2062707  1.2228552 #> 3          NA         NA         NA         NA         NA        NA         NA #> 4  0.66782843  0.3087139  0.8378206  0.3044256  0.8063030 0.2575057  0.4353962 #> 5 -0.02123811 -0.6503492  1.1686927 -0.8540634  1.0730075 0.4449248 -0.9429296 #> 6          NA         NA         NA         NA         NA        NA         NA #>        .m4f2     .m4f3      .m4f4      .m5f1      .m5f2     .m5f3       .m5f4 #> 1  0.1179912 1.6867161 -0.3149617  0.1303632  1.6548929 1.5959910 -0.69755418 #> 2 -2.1590779 0.7967761 -1.0174953 -2.1501813  1.2787053 0.7224308  0.39711553 #> 3         NA        NA         NA         NA         NA        NA          NA #> 4  0.7197966 0.5967368 -0.9248642  0.7224354  0.5193448 0.5700356  0.03266355 #> 5  1.1502964 0.1572142  0.5246276  1.1329384 -1.3643529 0.2357785  0.11707246 #> 6         NA        NA         NA         NA         NA        NA          NA #>        .m5f5 #> 1 -0.8091157 #> 2 -1.2663365 #> 3         NA #> 4 -1.0712061 #> 5  0.8709454 #> 6         NA

what: Plot a bass-ackwards diagram or per-level fit index chart вАФ autoplot.ackwards
title: Plot a bass-ackwards diagram or per-level fit index chart вАФ autoplot.ackwards
text: = "hierarchy" (default), renders layered bass-ackwards hierarchy ggplot2 diagram. Factors appear labelled boxes arranged levels (level 1 top, level k bottom default; set direction = "horizontal" left--right layout). -level edges cut_show hidden. Two aesthetics carry edge information, explained legend: sign (positive/negative) shown sign_by (edge colour default) magnitude (|r|) magnitude_by (line thickness default). aesthetic ever mapped without matching legend.

title: Plot a bass-ackwards diagram or per-level fit index chart вАФ autoplot.ackwards
code: # S3 method for class 'ackwards' autoplot(   object,   what = c("hierarchy", "fit"),   sign_by = c("color", "linetype", "both", "none"),   magnitude_by = c("linewidth", "none"),   cut_show = NULL,   color_pos = "#2166AC",   color_neg = "#D6604D",   color_edge = "black",   colour_pos = NULL,   colour_neg = NULL,   colour_edge = NULL,   node_width = 0.8,   node_height = 0.4,   min_sep = 1,   show_skip = NULL,   curvature = 0.2,   color_pruned = "grey80",   colour_pruned = NULL,   show_r = FALSE,   r_digits = 2L,   r_label_size = 2.5,   mono = FALSE,   edge_linewidth = NULL,   cut_strong = NULL,   direction = c("vertical", "horizontal"),   show_level_labels = TRUE,   level_label_size = 3,   node_labels = NULL,   primary_only = FALSE,   drop_pruned = FALSE,   compress_levels = FALSE,   show_arrows = TRUE,   legend = TRUE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'ackwards' plot(x, ...)

title: Plot a bass-ackwards diagram or per-level fit index chart вАФ autoplot.ackwards
text: object ackwards object. One "hierarchy" (default) "fit". Controls visualisation produced. parameters ignored = "fit". sign_by edge sign (positive vs negative correlation) encoded. One "color" (default; positive = color_pos, negative = color_neg), "linetype" (positive = solid, negative = dashed), "" (colour linetype, negative drawn distinct double-dash reads clearly greyscale), "none" (sign encoded; edges color_edge solid). Whichever channel used gets "Direction" legend. Colour default reads sign pre-attentively leaves linetype free; switch "linetype" "" greyscale colour-blind-safe figures. magnitude_by edge magnitude (|r|) encoded. One "linewidth" (default; thicker = stronger, |r| legend) "none" (constant width). numeric edge_linewidth also forces constant width value. cut_show Edges |r| < cut_show drawn. Defaults value used object created (x$meta$cut_show, typically 0.3). color_pos, colour_pos Colour positive edges sign_by uses colour. Default "#2166AC" (blue). colour_pos accepted British alias. color_neg, colour_neg Colour negative edges sign_by uses colour. Default "#D6604D" (red). colour_neg accepted British alias. color_edge, colour_edge Single colour edges sign_by use colour ("linetype" "none"). Default "black". colour_edge accepted British alias. node_width Width factor boxes layout units. Default 0.8. node_height Height factor boxes layout units. Default 0.4. min_sep Minimum horizontal separation nodes; passed ba_layout(). Default 1.0. show_skip Whether draw skip-level (non-adjacent) edges. NULL (default) auto-detects: TRUE object run pairs = "", FALSE otherwise. Ignored drop_pruned = TRUE. curvature Curvature skip-level edge arcs. Passed ggplot2::geom_curve(). Positive values curve right; default 0.2. Ignored drop_pruned = TRUE. color_pruned, colour_pruned Fill colour nodes flagged pruned/redundant. Default "grey80". applied object carries pruning annotations (x$prune non-NULL) drop_pruned = FALSE (pruned nodes omitted entirely drop_pruned = TRUE). colour_pruned accepted British alias. show_r Whether label drawn edge correlation coefficient. Default FALSE. TRUE, labels formatted APA style (leading zero stripped, e.g. .23, -.30) placed beside edge using white-background label clears arrowhead. r_digits Number decimal places edge labels show_r = TRUE. title: Compute between-level factor-score correlations вАФ compute_edges
text: R input correlation matrix D_x = diag(W_x' R W_x) actual score variances (assumed 1). avoids materialising scores remaining exact PCA, EFA (regression / Bartlett / tenBerge) вАУ produce linear score maps. algebra used (nonlinear scoring, missing R, user forces edge_method = "scores"), scores materialised data instead.

what: Glance at an ackwards object вАФ glance.ackwards
title: Glance at an ackwards object вАФ glance.ackwards
text: Returns one-row data frame top-level model metadata. EFA ESEM objects, fit indices deepest converged level included. five columns (CFI, TLI, RMSEA, SRMR, BIC) present across engines; columns unavailable given engine estimator NA (e.g., CFI SRMR NA EFA; five NA PCA; ESEM, BIC NA estimator = "WLSMV"/"ULSMV" вАУ limited-information estimators proper log-likelihood вАУ populated "ML"/"MLR"). scaled-test estimator ("WLSMV"/"ULSMV"/"MLR") CFI/TLI/RMSEA reported scaled variants (see tidy.ackwards() rationale).

title: Generate a node-label scaffold for autoplot вАФ label_template
text: named character vector: names factor IDs ("m{k}f{j}"), values label strings chosen style. vector suitable direct use node_labels argument autoplot.ackwards(). c(...) literal also printed console copy-paste.

title: Generate a node-label scaffold for autoplot вАФ label_template
text: factor IDs returned left--right, top--bottom order used ba_layout() autoplot.ackwards(), printed literal maps directly onto diagram.

title: Generate a node-label scaffold for autoplot вАФ label_template
text: "id" (default) вАУ every value equals factor ID ("m1f1", "m2f1", ...). round-trip -op: passing result node_labels without editing reproduces default labels exactly. Useful starting point adding substantive labels. "forbes" вАУ values follow Forbes (2023) convention: level-letter + within-level index ("A1", "B1", "B2", ...). Level 1 -> , level 2 -> B, level 3 -> C, . Within-level indices assigned canonical layout order (left right). Requires k_max <= 26 (LETTERS 26 entries); error raised deeper objects. "blank" вАУ values empty strings. Useful starting scaffold want supply every label scratch defaults showing .

title: Generate a node-label scaffold for autoplot вАФ label_template
code: x <- ackwards(bfi25, k_max = 5) #> Warning: ! 125 rows have missing values; correlations are computed pairwise. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` for consistent complete-case analysis.  # Start from ID defaults, then fill in your own labels: labs <- label_template(x) #> `label_template()` scaffold (id style): #> c( #>   "m1f1" = "m1f1", #>   "m2f1" = "m2f1", #>   "m2f2" = "m2f2", #>   "m3f1" = "m3f1", #>   "m3f2" = "m3f2", #>   "m3f3" = "m3f3", #>   "m4f1" = "m4f1", #>   "m4f2" = "m4f2", #>   "m4f3" = "m4f3", #>   "m4f4" = "m4f4", #>   "m5f1" = "m5f1", #>   "m5f2" = "m5f2", #>   "m5f3" = "m5f3", #>   "m5f4" = "m5f4", #>   "m5f5" = "m5f5" #> ) labs["m5f1"] <- "Neuroticism"  # Forbes letter convention: label_template(x, style = "forbes") #> `label_template()` scaffold (forbes style): #> c( #>   "m1f1" = "A1", #>   "m2f1" = "B1", #>   "m2f2" = "B2", #>   "m3f1" = "C1", #>   "m3f2" = "C2", #>   "m3f3" = "C3", #>   "m4f1" = "D1", #>   "m4f2" = "D2", #>   "m4f3" = "D3", #>   "m4f4" = "D4", #>   "m5f1" = "E1", #>   "m5f2" = "E2", #>   "m5f3" = "E3", #>   "m5f4" = "E4", #>   "m5f5" = "E5" #> )

what: Score new observations with a fitted ackwards model вАФ predict.ackwards
title: Score new observations with a fitted ackwards model вАФ predict.ackwards
text: Applies fitted bass-ackwards model new data вАФ example held-test split cross-validation design вАФ producing factor scores every level without retraining. idiomatic predict() front door machinery augment.ackwards(): call predict(object, newdata) returns exactly augment(object, data = newdata, append = FALSE), data frame holding .m{k}f{j} score columns, one row per row newdata.

title: Score new observations with a fitted ackwards model вАФ predict.ackwards
code: # S3 method for class 'ackwards' predict(object, newdata, scaling = c("fit", "sample"), ...)

title: Score new observations with a fitted ackwards model вАФ predict.ackwards
text: object ackwards object. newdata data frame numeric matrix variables (columns) used fit object. Required вАФ retrieve scores stored fit time, use augment(object) instead. scaling item means/SDs standardize newdata: "fit" (default, training moments) "sample" (newdata's moments). See augment.ackwards(). ... Ignored.

title: Score new observations with a fitted ackwards model вАФ predict.ackwards
text: data frame factor scores (columns .m{k}f{j}, one per factor per level), one row per row newdata original order.

title: Score new observations with a fitted ackwards model вАФ predict.ackwards
text: default scaling = "fit", newdata standardized fit-time item means/SDs stored object stored weight matrices applied, new scores land metric training solution: observation's score depend observations share split, train test scores directly comparable. See augment.ackwards() (section Scoring new observations) full semantics, scaling = "sample" alternative, non-Pearson-basis caveat. newdata must contain variables model fit (matched column name, extra columns ignored; bare unnamed matrix matched positionally). Rows missing items produce NA scores (scoring impute).

title: Score new observations with a fitted ackwards model вАФ predict.ackwards
code: # Cross-validation: fit on a training split, score the test split train <- bfi25[1:500, ] test <- bfi25[501:1000, ] x <- ackwards(train, k_max = 5) #> Warning: ! 58 rows have missing values; correlations are computed pairwise. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` for consistent complete-case analysis. test_scores <- predict(x, test) #> Warning: ! 67 rows contain missing values and will produce NA scores. #> вДє Score projection applies weights row-wise and propagates NAs listwise; FIML #>   estimation does not impute missing item responses. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` when fitting (so the model and scores share the #>   same complete rows), or call `na.omit(data)` before scoring. head(test_scores) #>         .m1f1      .m2f1      .m2f2      .m3f1      .m3f2     .m3f3      .m4f1 #> 1  1.31327917  1.2430095  0.4608252  0.6680509  0.1248161 1.4375648  0.7088025 #> 2  0.16529258  1.2321417 -1.9494198  1.0643443 -2.0400056 0.2062707  1.2228552 #> 3          NA         NA         NA         NA         NA        NA         NA #> 4  0.66782843  0.3087139  0.8378206  0.3044256  0.8063030 0.2575057  0.4353962 #> 5 -0.02123811 -0.6503492  1.1686927 -0.8540634  1.0730075 0.4449248 -0.9429296 #> 6          NA         NA         NA         NA         NA        NA         NA #>        .m4f2     .m4f3      .m4f4      .m5f1      .m5f2     .m5f3       .m5f4 #> 1  0.1179912 1.6867161 -0.3149617  0.1303632  1.6548929 1.5959910 -0.69755418 #> 2 -2.1590779 0.7967761 -1.0174953 -2.1501813  1.2787053 0.7224308  0.39711553 #> 3         NA        NA         NA         NA         NA        NA          NA #> 4  0.7197966 0.5967368 -0.9248642  0.7224354  0.5193448 0.5700356  0.03266355 #> 5  1.1502964 0.1572142  0.5246276  1.1329384 -1.3643529 0.2357785  0.11707246 #> 6         NA        NA         NA         NA         NA        NA          NA #>        .m5f5 #> 1 -0.8091157 #> 2 -1.2663365 #> 3         NA #> 4 -1.0712061 #> 5  0.8709454 #> 6         NA  # Identical to the augment() spelling: identical(test_scores, augment(x, data = test, append = FALSE)) #> Warning: ! 67 rows contain missing values and will produce NA scores. #> вДє Score projection applies weights row-wise and propagates NAs listwise; FIML #>   estimation does not impute missing item responses. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` when fitting (so the model and scores share the #>   same complete rows), or call `na.omit(data)` before scoring. #> [1] TRUE

what: Flag redundant or artifactual factors (Forbes 2023 extension) вАФ prune
title: Flag redundant or artifactual factors (Forbes 2023 extension) вАФ prune
text: prune() never removes anything ackwards object вАУ annotates factors pruned/prune_reason flags x$prune$nodes (flag-, never removes; object keeps every level). separate, cheap step extraction, can re-prune new thresholds without re-running ackwards(): prune() S3 generic (rather plain function) coexists prune generics already defined recursive-partitioning packages (e.g. rpart::prune) regardless package load order.
code: x <- ackwards(bfi25, k_max = 6, engine = "esem")  # expensive   x |> prune("redundant")                            # cheap, repeatable   x |> prune("redundant", redundancy_r = 0.95)        # no re-extraction

title: Flag redundant or artifactual factors (Forbes 2023 extension) вАФ prune
code: prune(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'ackwards' prune(   x,   rules = "none",   manual = NULL,   redundancy_r = 0.9,   redundancy_phi = NULL,   min_items = 3L,   orphan_r = 0.5,   ... )

title: Flag redundant or artifactual factors (Forbes 2023 extension) вАФ prune
text: x ackwards object. ... Reserved future methods/arguments. rules Character vector controlling auto-rules run. Default "none" (auto rule; combine manual pure manual pruning, call prune(x) arguments clear existing pruning). Options: "redundant" вАУ identify chains factors connected primary-parent links |r| >= redundancy_r (optionally phi > redundancy_phi). Applies Forbes's (2023) retention rule: keep bottom node chain reaches level k_max (specific); keep top node otherwise. Flagged nodes get pruned = TRUE prune_reason = "redundant" x$prune$nodes. "artifact" (alias "artefact", normalized "artifact") вАУ compute Tucker's congruence coefficient (phi) cross-level factor pairs store x$prune$phi, plus structural signals (few_items/orphan/split_merge) x$prune$structural. factors auto-flagged; artifact identification requires judgment (Forbes, 2023; Wicherts et al., 2016). manual Character vector factor labels (e.g. c("m4f3", "m4f4")) flag directly, addition instead auto rule. Standalone manual pruning supported: prune(x, manual = c("m4f3")). Unknown labels error. node already flagged auto rule keeps prune_reason; otherwise-unflagged manual nodes get prune_reason = "manual". redundancy_r Scalar (0, 1]. Adjacent primary-parent |r| threshold redundancy chains. Default 0.9 (Forbes, 2023). redundancy_phi Scalar (0, 1], NULL (default, auto), NA (explicit opt-). NULL: x$engine == "pca" вАУ phi filter. Component scores determinate (exact linear functions data, factor-score indeterminacy), score correlation |r| true correlation components ; phi adds nothing |r| already capture. x$engine "efa" "esem" вАУ automatically set 0.95 (Lorenzo-Seva & ten Berge, 2006). Factor-score indeterminacy -PCA means |r|-alone liberal; conjunctive phi criterion conservative default. cli message announces resolved value. Pass NA disable phi filtering regardless engine. Pass numeric value override engine. min_items Minimum number items factor must primary loader (highest |loading|). Factors fewer min_items primary items flagged few_items = TRUE x$prune$structural. used rules includes "artifact". Default 3L вАУ factor defined one two items -identified frequently extraction artifact rather replicable construct (classic "three-indicator rule"; Forbes, 2023, Fig. 2). orphan_r Threshold orphan structural signal. factor whose maximum adjacent-level |r| (immediately shallower deeper levels) falls orphan_r flagged orphan = TRUE x$prune$structural вАУ connect neighbouring solutions replicate across hierarchy. used rules includes "artifact". Default 0.5 вАУ moderate correlation; factor shares less quarter variance every neighbour structural outlier worth inspecting.

title: Flag redundant or artifactual factors (Forbes 2023 extension) вАФ prune
text: x, $prune populated (replacing prior pruning).

title: Flag redundant or artifactual factors (Forbes 2023 extension) вАФ prune
text: Forbes, M. K. (2023). Improving hierarchical models individual differences: extension Goldberg's bass-ackward method. Psychological Methods. doi:10.1037/met0000546 Lorenzo-Seva, U., & ten Berge, J. M. F. (2006). Tucker's congruence coefficient meaningful index factor similarity. Methodology, 2(2), 57вАУ64. doi:10.1027/1614-2241.2.2.57

title: Flag redundant or artifactual factors (Forbes 2023 extension) вАФ prune
code: x <- ackwards(bfi25, k_max = 5) #> Warning: ! 125 rows have missing values; correlations are computed pairwise. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` for consistent complete-case analysis.  xp <- prune(x, "redundant") #> вДє Redundancy pruning (|r| вЙ• 0.9) flagged 7 nodes. #> вДє Nodes are retained in the object; inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #>   `x$prune$chains`. xp$prune$nodes #>      id level pruned prune_reason #> 1  m1f1     1  FALSE         <NA> #> 2  m2f1     2  FALSE         <NA> #> 3  m2f2     2   TRUE    redundant #> 4  m3f1     3   TRUE    redundant #> 5  m3f2     3   TRUE    redundant #> 6  m3f3     3  FALSE         <NA> #> 7  m4f1     4   TRUE    redundant #> 8  m4f2     4   TRUE    redundant #> 9  m4f3     4   TRUE    redundant #> 10 m4f4     4   TRUE    redundant #> 11 m5f1     5  FALSE         <NA> #> 12 m5f2     5  FALSE         <NA> #> 13 m5f3     5  FALSE         <NA> #> 14 m5f4     5  FALSE         <NA> #> 15 m5f5     5  FALSE         <NA>  # Re-prune with a new threshold -- no re-extraction needed prune(x, "redundant", redundancy_r = 0.95) #> вДє Redundancy pruning (|r| вЙ• 0.95) flagged 6 nodes. #> вДє Nodes are retained in the object; inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #>   `x$prune$chains`. #>  #> вФАвФА Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: pearson #> n: 1,000 #> k (max): 5 #>  #> вФАвФА Levels вФАвФА #>  #> вЬФ k = 1: 1 factor, 20.6% variance #> вЬФ k = 2: 2 factors, 31.8% variance #> вЬФ k = 3: 3 factors, 40.1% variance #> вЬФ k = 4: 4 factors, 47.3% variance #> вЬФ k = 5: 5 factors, 53.3% variance #>  #> вФАвФА Edges вФАвФА #>  #> 14 of 40 edges have |r| вЙ• 0.3 #>  #> вФАвФА Pruning вФАвФА #>  #> Redundancy (|r| вЙ• 0.95): 6 nodes flagged #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: Pruning is interpretive relabeling, not re-estimation. Flagged nodes #> remain in the object; all edges are preserved. Inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #> `tidy(x, what = "nodes")`. #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. Per-level fit indices #> (EFA/ESEM) describe how well a k-factor model fits the items at that level -- #> they do not validate the edges or the hierarchy itself.  # Manual pruning: standalone, or mixed with an auto rule prune(x, manual = "m4f2") #>  #> вФАвФА Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: pearson #> n: 1,000 #> k (max): 5 #>  #> вФАвФА Levels вФАвФА #>  #> вЬФ k = 1: 1 factor, 20.6% variance #> вЬФ k = 2: 2 factors, 31.8% variance #> вЬФ k = 3: 3 factors, 40.1% variance #> вЬФ k = 4: 4 factors, 47.3% variance #> вЬФ k = 5: 5 factors, 53.3% variance #>  #> вФАвФА Edges вФАвФА #>  #> 14 of 40 edges have |r| вЙ• 0.3 #>  #> вФАвФА Pruning вФАвФА #>  #> Manual: 1 node explicitly flagged (m4f2) #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: Pruning is interpretive relabeling, not re-estimation. Flagged nodes #> remain in the object; all edges are preserved. Inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #> `tidy(x, what = "nodes")`. #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. Per-level fit indices #> (EFA/ESEM) describe how well a k-factor model fits the items at that level -- #> they do not validate the edges or the hierarchy itself. prune(x, "redundant", manual = "m4f2") #> вДє Redundancy pruning (|r| вЙ• 0.9) flagged 7 nodes. #> вДє Nodes are retained in the object; inspect with `x$prune$nodes` and #>   `x$prune$chains`. #>  #> вФАвФА Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: pearson #> n: 1,000 #> k (max): 5 #>  #> вФАвФА Levels вФАвФА #>  #> вЬФ k = 1: 1 factor, 20.6% variance #> вЬФ k = 2: 2 factors, 31.8% variance #> вЬФ k = 3: 3 factors, 40.1% variance #> вЬФ k = 4: 4 factors, 47.3% variance #> вЬФ k = 5: 5 factors, 53.3% variance #>  #> вФАвФА Edges вФАвФА #>  #> 14 of 40 edges have |r| вЙ• 0.3 #>  #> вФАвФА Pruning вФАвФА #>  #> Redundancy (|r| вЙ• 0.9): 7 nodes flagged #> Manual: 1 node explicitly flagged (m4f2) #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: Pruning is interpretive relabeling, not re-estimation. title: Simulated continuous bass-ackwards teaching example (16 items, known hierarchy) вАФ sim16
text: Population model. Sigma = Lambda %*% Phi %*% t(Lambda) + Psi, oblique common-factor model 4 true group factors, sampled via Cholesky factorization (base R; MASS dependency): items load 0.75 true factor (cross-loadings). Factor correlations: 0.45 within metatrait (f1-f2, f3-f4), 0.15 metatraits. Uniquenesses 1 - communality (uniform 0.4375 symmetry design ). Ground-truth hierarchy (verified ackwards(engine = "efa")): k=1 recovers single general factor across 16 items; k=2 splits along metatrait line (i1-i8 vs. i9-i16); k=4 recovers 4 true group factors exactly; six suggest_k() recommendations (five criteria вАУ VSS reports complexities 1 2) reach consensus k = 4. Idealized design. planted signal strong clean, six suggest_k() recommendations converge k = 4 вАУ deliberately easy case, building intuition recovering known hierarchy looks like. Real data rarely agree cleanly: bfi25 criteria span k = 4вАУ6. two datasets complementary teaching foils вАУ sim16 "watch method recover structure planted," bfi25 "reason hierarchy criteria disagree." Present sim16's consensus ideal, norm. Deliberate overextraction artifact k=5. population exactly 4 factors, requesting 5th finds real dimension: EFA produces orphan factor zero primary-loading items. prune(x, "artifact") (default min_items = 3, orphan_r = 0.5), factor flagged few_items orphan. true (non- splitting) factors persist essentially unchanged k=3 onward, parent-child score correlations approach 1 flagged prune(x, "redundant") (|r| >= .9 , EFA auto-default, Tucker's phi > .95). textbook overextraction artifact, included Forbes/redundancy examples guaranteed finding teach (unlike bfi25, reliably trigger one). regenerate dataset, run source("data-raw/sim16.R") package root (set.seed(42)).

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis вАФ suggest_k
code: suggest_k(   data,   k_max = NULL,   criteria = c("pa_pc", "pa_fa", "map", "vss", "cd"),   cor = "pearson",   n_obs = NULL,   n_iter = 20L,   seed = NULL,   ... )

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis вАФ suggest_k
text: data data frame numeric matrix (items columns, observations rows). Alternatively, pre-computed correlation matrix may supplied (square, symmetric, numeric matrix unit diagonal). correlation matrix supplied, n_obs required (PA VSS need N), cor argument ignored, Comparison Data (CD) criterion skipped (CD requires raw item distributions resampling). k_max Maximum number factors/components evaluate recommending depth вАУ depth . Defaults min(ncol(data) - 1, 8). Increase expect deeper hierarchy. (Note: distinct meaning ackwards()'s k_max, extraction depth; two share name dial suggest_k() -> ackwards() workflow, just applied different stages.) criteria Character vector criteria compute. subset c("pa_pc", "pa_fa", "map", "vss", "cd"); default five. "pa_pc" "pa_fa" share one parallel-analysis call (neither fast); "map" "vss" share one VSS call. Criteria requested skipped entirely (computation) k_* fields result NA. "vss" selects VSS-1 VSS-2 unit. cor Correlation basis: "pearson" (default) "spearman". match cor argument plan use ackwards(). Ignored data correlation matrix (basis already fixed). n_obs Number observations. Required data pre-computed correlation matrix (PA VSS need N). Ignored raw data supplied (N determined nrow(data)). n_iter Number Monte Carlo iterations parallel analysis. Default 20. Reduce 5 fast/exploratory runs; increase 100+ publication. seed Integer seed passed set.seed() Comparison Data (CD) step. NULL (default) uses current RNG state. Note: parallel-analysis step uses psych::fa.parallel(), respond reliably set.seed() вАУ PA simulation results vary across calls regardless seed. ... Reserved future arguments.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis вАФ suggest_k
text: object class "suggest_k". Print formatted summary; call autoplot() diagnostic scree/criteria plot. list contains: k_parallel_pc Recommended k PC-based parallel analysis (NA_integer_ "pa_pc" criteria). k_parallel_fa Recommended k FA-based parallel analysis (NA_integer_ FA factor exceeded random threshold, "pa_fa" criteria). k_map Recommended k MAP (NA_integer_ "map" criteria). k_vss1 Recommended k VSS complexity-1 (NA_integer_ "vss" criteria). k_vss2 Recommended k VSS complexity-2 (NA_integer_ "vss" criteria). k_cd Recommended k Comparison Data (NA_integer_ "cd" criteria, EFAtools installed, CD fails). cd_available Logical; TRUE "cd" requested, EFAtools found, CD ran successfully. criteria Data frame one row per k: k, ev_obs (observed PC eigenvalue), ev_obs_fa (observed FA eigenvalue), pa_pc_quant / pa_fa_quant (95th-pct simulated eigenvalue basis), pa_pc_suggested / pa_fa_suggested (logical retention), map, vss1, vss2. Columns non-requested criteria NA. criteria_requested Character vector criteria requested (therefore computed). k_max, n_obs, n_vars, cor Metadata.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis вАФ suggest_k
text: Criteria available (controlled criteria argument): PA-PC ("pa_pc") вАУ Horn (1965) parallel analysis PC eigenvalues. Compares observed eigenvalues random correlation matrices; suggests retaining components whose eigenvalues exceed 95th percentile chance. Tends overextract; treat upper bound. PA-FA ("pa_fa") вАУ Horn (1965) parallel analysis using common-factor eigenvalues. conservative PA-PC better match EFA ESEM engines ackwards(). Shares one psych::fa.parallel() call PA-PC. MAP ("map") вАУ Velicer (1976) Minimum Average Partial criterion. Finds k minimises average squared partial correlation remaining extracting k components. Usually conservative. Shares one psych::vss() call VSS. VSS ("vss") вАУ Revelle & Rocklin (1979) Simple Structure fit complexities 1 2 (VSS-1 VSS-2). Finds k maximising fit simple loading structure. Shares one psych::vss() call MAP. CD ("cd") вАУ Ruscio & Roche (2012) Comparison Data. Resamples observed item distributions generate comparison eigenvalue profiles; retains factors adding one longer improves RMSE beyond chance. Requires EFAtools package (install separately). Skipped informational message EFAtools absent correlation matrix supplied. PA (bases), MAP, VSS share correlation matrix ackwards(). CD operates raw data matrix directly (required resampling).

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis вАФ suggest_k
text: k ackwards() maximum depth, claim exactly k factors exist. Users commonly set k one two levels consensus watch higher-level factors fragment вАУ feature method, overextraction.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis вАФ suggest_k
text: PA-PC particular tends recommend factors replicate across independent samples, especially correlated items (Forbes, 2023). PA-FA CD conservative. Treat full set criteria range: true k likely somewhere middle.

title: Suggest a maximum number of factors for bass-ackwards analysis вАФ suggest_k
text: Forbes, M. K. (2023). Improving hierarchical models individual differences: extension Goldberg's bass-ackward method. Psychological Methods. doi:10.1037/met0000546 Horn, J. L. (1965). rationale test number factors factor analysis. Psychometrika, 30, 179вАУ185. Revelle, W., & Rocklin, T. (1979). simple structure: alternative procedure estimating optimal number interpretable factors. Multivariate Behavioral Research, 14(4), 403вАУ414. Ruscio, J., & Roche, B. (2012). Determining number factors retain exploratory factor analysis using comparison data known factorial structure. Psychological Assessment, 24(2), 282вАУ292. Velicer, W. F. (1976). Determining number components matrix partial correlations. Psychometrika, 41, 321вАУ327.

what: Summarise an ackwards object вАФ summary.ackwards
title: Summarise an ackwards object вАФ summary.ackwards
text: Returns structured summary_ackwards object , printed, shows per-level variance fit indices, readable lineage list, (present) pruning annotations. verbose print.ackwards(); designed inspection reporting.

title: Summarise an ackwards object вАФ summary.ackwards
code: # S3 method for class 'ackwards' summary(object, ...)

title: Summarise an ackwards object вАФ summary.ackwards
text: object ackwards object. ... Ignored.

title: Summarise an ackwards object вАФ summary.ackwards
text: object class "summary_ackwards", printed via print.summary_ackwards().

title: Summarise an ackwards object вАФ summary.ackwards
code: x <- ackwards(bfi25, k_max = 5) #> Warning: ! 125 rows have missing values; correlations are computed pairwise. #> вДє Use `missing = "listwise"` for consistent complete-case analysis. summary(x) #>  #> вФАвФА Summary: Bass-Ackwards Analysis (ackwards) вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Engine: pca #> Rotation: varimax #> Basis: pearson #> n: 1,000 #> k (max): 5 #>  #> вФАвФА Levels вФАвФА #>  #> k = 1: 1 factor (20.6% cumulative variance) #> m1f1 20.61% eigenvalue 5.15 #> k = 2: 2 factors (31.8% cumulative variance) #> m2f1 18.46% eigenvalue 5.15 #> m2f2 13.36% eigenvalue 2.8 #> k = 3: 3 factors (40.1% cumulative variance) #> m3f1 16.39% eigenvalue 5.15 #> m3f2 12.42% eigenvalue 2.8 #> m3f3 11.28% eigenvalue 2.07 #> k = 4: 4 factors (47.3% cumulative variance) #> m4f1 15.77% eigenvalue 5.15 #> m4f2 12.58% eigenvalue 2.8 #> m4f3 10.67% eigenvalue 2.07 #> m4f4 8.28% eigenvalue 1.8 #> k = 5: 5 factors (53.3% cumulative variance) #> m5f1 12.57% eigenvalue 5.15 #> m5f2 12.51% eigenvalue 2.8 #> m5f3 10.75% eigenvalue 2.07 #> m5f4 9.47% eigenvalue 1.8 #> m5f5 7.98% eigenvalue 1.5 #>  #> вФАвФА Lineage (primary parents) вФАвФА #>  #> m1f1 вЖТ m2f1, m2f2 #> m2f1 вЖТ m3f1, m3f3 #> m2f2 вЖТ m3f2 #> m3f1 вЖТ m4f1 #> m3f2 вЖТ m4f2 #> m3f3 вЖТ m4f3, m4f4 #> m4f1 вЖТ m5f1, m5f4 #> m4f2 вЖТ m5f2 #> m4f3 вЖТ m5f3 #> m4f4 вЖТ m5f5 #> вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА #> Note: This is a series of linked solutions, not a fitted hierarchical model. #> Cross-level edges are descriptive score correlations. Per-level fit indices #> (EFA/ESEM) describe how well a k-factor model fits the items at that level -- #> they do not validate the edges or the hierarchy itself.

what: Tidy an ackwards object into a long data frame вАФ tidy.ackwards
title: Tidy an ackwards object into a long data frame вАФ tidy.ackwards
text: Returns structured data ackwards object tidy format. default (= "edges") returns graph edge list drives diagrams.

title: Tidy an ackwards object into a long data frame вАФ tidy.ackwards
code: # S3 method for class 'ackwards' tidy(   x,   what = c("edges", "loadings", "variance", "fit", "nodes", "scores"),   primary_only = FALSE,   sort = c("none", "strength"),   format = c("long", "wide"),   conf_level = 0.95,   ... )

title: Tidy an ackwards object into a long data frame вАФ tidy.ackwards
text: x ackwards object. extract: "edges" (default) вАУ one row per directed -level edge: , , level_from, level_to, r, is_primary, above_cut. boot_edges() run object, four bootstrap columns appended: se, lo, hi (bootstrap standard error percentile confidence-interval endpoints), n_boot_ok (usable replicates). "loadings" вАУ one row per item x factor x level: level, factor, item, loading, se, ci_lower, ci_upper. se, ci_lower, ci_upper populated engine = "esem" (produces rotation-aware loading SEs); NA PCA EFA. confidence level controlled conf_level. "variance" вАУ one row per factor x level: level, factor, proportion, cumulative. proportions total item variance 0-1 scale (multiply 100 percentage). "fit" вАУ one row per fit statistic x level: level, statistic, value. PCA objects statistics eigenvalues; EFA objects chi, dof, p_value, RMSEA, TLI, BIC вАУ chi likelihood-ratio chi-square (psych::fa()'s STATISTIC), chi, dof, p_value, RMSEA, TLI share one statistical framing (psych's residual-based empirical chi-square different statistic reported); ESEM chi, dof, p_value, CFI, TLI, RMSEA, SRMR, BIC. ESEM scaled-test estimator ("WLSMV"/"ULSMV" ordinal data, "MLR" continuous), whole row вАУ chi/dof/ p_value CFI/TLI/RMSEA вАУ reports lavaan's mean--variance-adjusted ("scaled") variant, every quantity shares one scaling. matters WLSMV/ULSMV: naive chi-square valid reference distribution (lavaan's summary() labels p-value "Unknown"), naive CFI/TLI badly optimistic ordinal data (Xia & Yang, 2019). "ML" scaled variant, reports naive values (correct ones ML). SRMR scaled variant reported -. BIC NA WLSMV/ULSMV (proper log-likelihood limited-information estimator) populated ML/MLR. Use format = "wide" one row per non-anchor level (k >= 2; saturated 1-factor anchor dropped, matching summary() autoplot(= "fit")), one column per statistic. Conventional fit cutoffs (Hu & Bentler 1999) shown reference lines autoplot(= "fit") inline summary(), returned pass/fail column вАУ contested thresholds, report-, never gate anything (see functions' docs). format oriented EFA/ESEM model-fit statistics; PCA "statistics" per-component eigenvalues. "nodes" вАУ Forbes-extension pruning annotations (requires prune != "none" object created). One row per factor across levels: id, level, pruned, prune_reason. Returns empty data frame columns pruning applied. "scores" вАУ long-format per-observation factor scores (requires keep_scores = TRUE fit time use augment.ackwards() --fly computation). Columns: obs (row index), level, factor, score. primary_only = "edges" . TRUE, returns just factor's primary-parent edge (is_primary == TRUE) вАУ lineage tree diagram draws solid arrows. Default FALSE (edges). Errors value . sort = "edges" . One "none" (default, natural order) "strength" (descending |r|). Ignored values . format = "fit" . One "long" (default, one row per statistic x level) "wide" (one row per level, one column per statistic). Errors values . conf_level = "loadings" . Confidence level loading intervals; default 0.95. intervals computed loading ¬± qnorm((1 + conf_level) / 2) * se NA engines carry SEs (PCA, EFA). Errors values . ... Ignored.

what: Display the salient items for each factor вАФ top_items
title: Display the salient items for each factor вАФ top_items
text: Returns, per level factor, items whose absolute loading meets exceeds cut, sorted descending absolute loading. gives concise reading "factor " without printing full item--factor matrix, scale well large k many items.

title: Display the salient items for each factor вАФ top_items
code: top_items(   x,   level = NULL,   cut = 0.3,   n = NULL,   sort = TRUE,   by = c("factor", "item"),   show_labels = TRUE )

title: Display the salient items for each factor вАФ top_items
text: x ackwards object. level Integer vector selecting level(s) include. NULL (default) returns levels. cut Absolute-loading threshold. Items |loading| >= cut shown. Default 0.3, matching cut_show plotting default. n Maximum number items show per factor. NULL (default) shows items meeting cut. set, top-n items |loading| kept applying cut. sort Logical. TRUE (default), items within group ordered descending |loading|. Set FALSE keep original order (useful items meaningful sequence). One "factor" (default) "item". "factor" groups listing factor (salient items factor вАУ "factor ?"). "item" inverts grouping list, item, factors loads вАУ makes cross-loadings legible ("item go?"). n sort apply within whichever unit selects. show_labels Logical. TRUE (default) data carried variable-label attribute fit time (see ackwards()), items shown label (id); items without label fall back bare id. Set FALSE always show bare m{k}f{j}-style item ids.

title: Display the salient items for each factor вАФ top_items
text: object class "top_items". Print grouped cli listing. underlying data frame (one row per selected item) accessible via $data contains columns level, factor, item, loading (plus label labels available). values equal corresponding tidy(x, = "loadings") rows (filtering optional sorting).

title: Display the salient items for each factor вАФ top_items
text: Loadings signed reflect object's primary-parent sign alignment (see ackwards()). Items near cut threshold may appear one sign orientation ; expected informative.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: First public release. Licensed MIT.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Three engines available via engine argument ackwards(): PCA (engine = "pca") вАФ original Goldberg (2006) bass-ackwards method. Fastest; never fails converge; exact WвА≤RW algebra -level edges. EFA (engine = "efa") вАФ exploratory factor analysis via psych::fa() tenBerge scoring weights closed-form algebra path. ESEM (engine = "esem") вАФ EFA estimated SEM framework via lavaan::efa() WLSMV ordinal estimation, per-level fit indices (CFI, TLI, RMSEA, SRMR), rotation-aware standard errors. Required clinical/HiTOP workflow. engines use varimax (orthogonal) rotation вАФ rotation compatible WвА≤RW algebra. Oblique rotation unsupported design.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: suggest_k() runs five complementary retention criteria returns consensus range: PA-PC PA-FA вАФ parallel analysis PC FA eigenvalue bases (psych::fa.parallel(fa = "")). MAP (Velicer 1976) VSS-1/VSS-2 (Revelle & Rocklin 1979) via psych::vss(). Comparison Data (Ruscio & Roche 2012) via EFAtools::CD() вАФ skipped gracefully EFAtools installed. autoplot.suggest_k() produces ggplot2 diagnostic: four-panel 2√Ч2 grid (scree/PA, MAP, VSS, CD RMSE) EFAtools installed, three-panel single-column layout (scree/PA, MAP, VSS) otherwise. returned object also carries cd_rmse (column means CDвАЩs RMSE eigenvalue matrix) use CD panel.

what: Between-level edges вАФ exact WвА≤RW algebra
title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: -level factor-score correlations computed via closed-form WвА≤RW algebra linear engines (PCA, EFA, ESEM tenBerge weights) вАФ scores need materialized. Standardization uses real score SDs sqrt(diag(WвА≤RW)) rather assuming unit variance. materialized-scores fallback available cross-check test verifies two routes agree within tolerance.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Pass cor = "polychoric" use polychoric correlations basis PCA EFA. ESEM, lavaanвАЩs polychoric matrix used edge computation (consistent fitted model). WLSMV estimator default cor = "polychoric" ESEM. ackwards() detects likely-ordinal columns (вЙ§ 7 distinct integer values) emits suppressible warning cor = "pearson" set.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Three strategies via missing ackwards(): "pairwise" (default) вАФ pairwise-complete correlations; warns NAs present. "listwise" вАФ complete cases , consistent N across fit edges. "fiml" вАФ Full Information ML via lavaan (ESEM ML/MLR ); edges derived FIML saturated model.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: pairs = "" computes -level correlations every level pair, revealing skip-level associations. prune = "redundant" flags chains adjacent |r| вЙ• 0.9 (Forbes retention rule). prune = "artefact" computes TuckerвАЩs ѕЖ researcher inspection; auto-flagging. autoplot() renders pruned views (drop_pruned = TRUE) skip-level arcs.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: ggplot2 layered diagram extensive customization: edge color sign, width/alpha |r|, solid/dashed strength; monochrome mode (mono); APA-formatted correlation labels (show_r, r_digits, r_label_size); custom node labels (node_labels); primary-mode (primary_only); level axis labels (show_level_labels); arrowhead toggle (show_arrows); fixed edge width (edge_linewidth); legend toggle (legend); Forbes-style pruned view (drop_pruned, compress_levels).

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: top_items(x, level, cut, n, sort) вАФ lists salient items factor, filtered |loading| >= cut (default 0.3) sorted descending absolute loading. Arguments level, n, sort allow subsetting levels, capping items per factor, controlling order. Returns top_items S3 object grouped cli print method. label_template(x, style) вАФ generates named-character-vector scaffold autoplot(x, node_labels = ...). Styles: "id" (default), "forbes" (level-letter + within-level index: "A1", "B1", "B2", вА¶), "blank". Factor IDs canonical layout order; editable c(...) literal also printed copy-paste. See vignette("ackwards-interpret") end--end naming workflow: reading factor top_items(), hierarchy-aware naming (parent vs.¬†child, blends), sign-alignment caveat, top_items() вЖТ label_template() вЖТ autoplot(node_labels = ...) round-trip.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: psych moved Imports вАФ default PCA EFA engines require psych; placing Suggests meant mandatory install prompt core functionality. SEM (lavaan) plotting (ggplot2) stacks remain Suggests. GPArotation removed вАФ varimax rotation routes base stats::varimax; GPArotation never loaded supported path. bfi25 example dataset bundled вАФ 1 000-row, 25-item subset SAPA/IPIP Big Five data (sampled psych::bfi). Used throughout examples vignettes run without reaching psychвАЩs namespace. See ?bfi25 provenance @source.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: ackwards() suggest_k() now accept pre-computed correlation matrix place raw item data, detected automatically matrix shape (square, symmetric, unit diagonal). Engine gating вАФ "pca" "efa" supported; "esem" errors clearly (lavaan requires raw data WLSMV estimation per-level fit indices). n_obs argument вАФ new optional argument functions. Required engine = "efa" matrix (psych needs N chi-square/RMSEA/TLI); optional engine = "pca" (stored NA omitted). Ignored (warning) raw data supplied. Edge correctness вАФ edges ackwards(R, ...) match ackwards(data, ...) exactly correlation matrix: routes use closed-form W'RW algebra. cor missing arguments вАФ ignored (warning set) matrix input. $cor stored NA_character_ printed "(user-supplied matrix)". Score paths blocked вАФ keep_scores = TRUE, augment(), tidy(= "scores") error clearly: individual-level scoring requires row-level item responses. CD skipped suggest_k() вАФ Comparison Data resamples raw item distributions skipped info note matrix supplied. Validation вАФ square, numeric, finite, symmetric, unit diagonal, |r| <= 1, NA; synthesises V1..Vp dimnames absent; warns (auto-smooth) positive-definite; errors clear message covariance matrix detected.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: print.ackwards() вАФ compact cli summary card. summary.ackwards() вАФ per-level variance, fit indices, lineage list, pruning notes. tidy(x, = "edges" / "loadings" / "loadings_se" / "variance" / "nodes" / "scores") вАФ long-format tidy tibbles. = "edges", primary_only = TRUE returns just factorвАЩs primary-parent edge (lineage tree) sort = "strength" orders |r|. = "loadings_se" returns rotation-aware loading standard errors (ESEM ). glance(x) вАФ one-row model-level summary. augment(x, data = ...) вАФ appends factor scores data frame; recomputes stored weights scores materialized fit time.

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Vignette comparison tables reworked legibility: ackwards-engines ackwards-ordinal вАФ stacked long-format kable tables replaced wide gt tables: one row per item/edge, one column per engine/basis, plus explicit ќФ column teaching point (EFA/polychoric attenuation). Factor sign correspondence asserted differencing; edge tables surface primary-parent disagreements NA rather hiding . ackwards-forbes вАФ prune-nodes raw tidy() print replaced styled gt table; narrated counts (n_adj, n_all, n_redundant, top skip-edge) now computed via inline R expressions can never go stale. gt added Suggests (vignette table formatting ; never loaded core).

title: ackwards 0.1.0
text: Test suite reaches 100% line coverage. Genuinely unreachable defensive branches (engine fallbacks, lavaan error/warning handlers) excluded via # nocov rather brittle environment-dependent tests.
